channels</strong> (1-14 on 2.4GHz, 36-165 on 5GHz if supported)</li> <li>Sends results to Orbic via shared storage</li> <li>DagShell logs to <strong>wardriver CSV</strong></li> </ul> <p><strong>Coordination</strong>:</p> <ul> <li>Pi and Orbic <strong>synchronize</strong> on GPS timestamp</li> <li>Prevents duplicate entries</li> <li>Combines <strong>both scan sources</strong> in single log</li> </ul> <h4 id="deauth-attacks"> <a href="#deauth-attacks" title="Deauth Attacks">Deauth Attacks</a> <a href="#deauth-attacks" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Deauth Attacks">#</a> </h4> <p><strong>Purpose</strong>: Disconnect clients from WiFi networks (for lab testing)</p> <p><strong>How It Works</strong>:</p> <ul> <li>Sends <strong>802.11 deauthentication frames</strong></li> <li>Spoofs <strong>AP’s MAC address</strong></li> <li>Client receives “disconnect” from AP</li> <li>Client <strong>drops connection</strong> and attempts reconnect</li> </ul> <p><strong>Control Methods</strong>:</p> <ul> <li><strong>One-shot deauth</strong>: Single burst of frames</li> <li><strong>Continuous deauth</strong>: Blocks reconnection (DoS)</li> <li><strong>Remote control</strong>: Triggered from DagShell scan page</li> </ul> <p><strong>Use Cases</strong> (Authorized):</p> <ul> <li><strong>Rogue device removal</strong> from own network</li> <li><strong>Penetration testing</strong> with permission</li> <li><strong>Evil Twin attacks</strong> in lab (force clients to fake AP)</li> </ul> <p><strong>⚠️ Legal Warning</strong>: Deauth attacks are <strong>illegal against networks you don’t own</strong>. FCC violations + potential CFAA charges.</p> <h4 id="auto-start-service"> <a href="#auto-start-service" title="Auto-Start Service">Auto-Start Service</a> <a href="#auto-start-service" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Auto-Start Service">#</a> </h4> <p><strong>systemd Service</strong>: Pi companion auto-starts on boot</p> <p><strong>Service File</strong> (<code>/etc/systemd/system/pi-companion.service</code>):</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-ini" data-lang="ini"><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#66d9ef">[Unit]</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">Description</span><span style="color:#f92672">=</span><span style="color:#e6db74">DagShell Pi Companion</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">After</span><span style="color:#f92672">=</span><span style="color:#e6db74">network.target</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#66d9ef">[Service]</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">ExecStart</span><span style="color:#f92672">=</span><span style="color:#e6db74">/usr/bin/python3 /opt/dagshell/pi_companion.py</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">Restart</span><span style="color:#f92672">=</span><span style="color:#e6db74">always</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">User</span><span style="color:#f92672">=</span><span style="color:#e6db74">root</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#66d9ef">[Install]</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">WantedBy</span><span style="color:#f92672">=</span><span style="color:#e6db74">multi-user.target</span> </span></span></code></pre></div><p><strong>Commands</strong>:</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-bash" data-lang="bash"><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Enable auto-start</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>sudo systemctl enable pi-companion </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Start service</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>sudo systemctl start pi-companion </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Check status</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>sudo systemctl status pi-companion </span></span></code></pre></div><h4 id="oui-database"> <a href="#oui-database" title="OUI Database">OUI Database</a> <a href="#oui-database" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="OUI Database">#</a> </h4> <p><strong>Purpose</strong>: Lookup MAC address vendor/manufacturer</p> <p><strong>Database</strong>: <span itemscope itemtype="https://schema.org/WebPage"> <meta itemprop="name" content="OUI Master Database"> <meta itemprop="url" content="https://standards.ieee.org/products-programs/regauth/"> <a href="https://standards.ieee.org/products-programs/regauth/" rel="noopener external" target="_blank" >OUI Master Database</a> </span> prefix-based API</p> <p><strong>How It Works</strong>:</p> <ul> <li>Extracts <strong>first 3 bytes</strong> of MAC address (OUI prefix)</li> <li>Queries API: <code>api.example.com/oui/A4:83:E7</code></li> <li>Returns <strong>manufacturer name</strong></li> </ul> <p><strong>Example</strong>:</p> <pre tabindex="0"><code>MAC: A4:83:E7:12:34:56 OUI: A4:83:E7 Vendor: Apple, Inc. </code></pre><p><strong>Integration</strong>: Used by ARP scanner, BLE scanner, and WiFi wardriving</p> <h3 id="-file-explorer"> <a href="#-file-explorer" title="📁 File Explorer">📁 File Explorer</a> <a href="#-file-explorer" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="📁 File Explorer">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>Purpose</strong>: Browse and manage files on device</p> <p><strong>Location</strong>: <code>/data/</code> directory (writable partition)</p> <p><strong>Capabilities</strong>:</p> <ul> <li><strong>Browse files</strong> and subdirectories</li> <li><strong>Download</strong> files to your computer</li> <li><strong>Delete</strong> files (with confirmation)</li> <li><strong>View file sizes</strong> and timestamps</li> </ul> <p><strong>Common Files</strong>:</p> <ul> <li><code>wardrive_log.csv</code> - WiFi scan data</li> <li><code>bluetooth_scan.csv</code> - BLE device data</li> <li><code>gps_track.gpx</code> - GPS tracks</li> <li><code>dns_queries.log</code> - Captured DNS requests</li> <li><code>arp_scan.txt</code> - Network device list</li> </ul> <p><strong>Wigle Upload</strong>:</p> <ul> <li><strong>Direct browser upload</strong> from Files page</li> <li>Authenticates with WiGLE account</li> <li>Uploads CSV without downloading first</li> </ul> <hr> <h2 id="installation-guide"> <a href="#installation-guide" title="Installation Guide">Installation Guide</a> <a href="#installation-guide" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Installation Guide">#</a> </h2> <h3 id="prerequisites"> <a href="#prerequisites" title="Prerequisites">Prerequisites</a> <a href="#prerequisites" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Prerequisites">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>Hardware</strong>:</p> <ul> <li><strong>Orbic RCL400</strong> mobile hotspot</li> <li><strong>Computer</strong> (Windows, macOS, or Linux)</li> <li><strong>USB cable</strong> (if flashing firmware directly)</li> </ul> <p><strong>Software</strong>:</p> <ul> <li><strong>Python 3</strong> with <code>requests</code> and <code>cryptography</code> modules</li> <li><strong>Web browser</strong> (Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari)</li> </ul> <p><strong>Optional</strong>:</p> <ul> <li><strong>Raspberry Pi 3B+</strong> or newer (for GPS and extended features)</li> <li><strong>USB GPS dongle</strong> (U-Blox 7 chipset)</li> </ul> <h3 id="method-1-web-flasher-recommended"> <a href="#method-1-web-flasher-recommended" title="Method 1: Web Flasher (Recommended)">Method 1: Web Flasher (Recommended)</a> <a href="#method-1-web-flasher-recommended" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Method 1: Web Flasher (Recommended)">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>Easiest method</strong> - No command line required</p> <p><strong>Step 1</strong>: Visit DagShell Webflasher</p> <ul> <li>URL: <span itemscope itemtype="https://schema.org/WebPage"> <meta itemprop="name" content="dagnazty.github.io/DagShell/orbic.html"> <meta itemprop="url" content="https://dagnazty.github.io/DagShell/orbic.html"> <a href="https://dagnazty.github.io/DagShell/orbic.html" rel="noopener external" target="_blank" >dagnazty.github.io/DagShell/orbic.html</a> </span> </li> </ul> <p><strong>Step 2</strong>: Generate PKI Certificates</p> <ul> <li>Click <strong>“Generate Certificates”</strong> button</li> <li>Browser generates <strong>2-tier PKI</strong> (Root CA + Server certificate)</li> <li><strong>Download files</strong>: <code>root.der</code> and <code>server.der</code></li> </ul> <p><strong>Step 3</strong>: Enable Root Shell on Orbic</p> <ul> <li>Connect to Orbic WiFi network</li> <li>Enter <strong>admin password</strong> in web form</li> <li>Click <strong>“Enable Shell”</strong></li> <li>Web page exploits Orbic API to open <strong>root shell on port 24</strong></li> </ul> <p><strong>Step 4</strong>: Deploy Firmware</p> <ul> <li>Click <strong>“Deploy DagShell”</strong> button</li> <li>Script uploads: <ul> <li><code>orbic_app</code> (main firmware binary)</li> <li><code>root.der</code> (root certificate)</li> <li><code>server.der</code> (TLS server certificate)</li> <li><code>dagshell_boot.sh</code> (persistence script)</li> </ul> </li> <li>Installation takes <strong>2-3 minutes</strong></li> </ul> <p><strong>Step 5</strong>: Reboot Orbic</p> <ul> <li>Power cycle the device</li> <li>DagShell auto-starts on boot</li> </ul> <p><strong>Step 6</strong>: Access DagShell</p> <ul> <li>Open browser to: <code>https://192.168.1.1:8443/</code></li> <li>Accept <strong>security warning</strong> (self-signed certificate - this is expected)</li> <li>Login with default credentials (if prompted)</li> </ul> <h3 id="method-2-manual-installation"> <a href="#method-2-manual-installation" title="Method 2: Manual Installation">Method 2: Manual Installation</a> <a href="#method-2-manual-installation" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Method 2: Manual Installation">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>For advanced users</strong> who want to build from source</p> <h4 id="step-1-install-dependencies"> <a href="#step-1-install-dependencies" title="Step 1: Install Dependencies">Step 1: Install Dependencies</a> <a href="#step-1-install-dependencies" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Step 1: Install Dependencies">#</a> </h4> <p><strong>Windows</strong>:</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-powershell" data-lang="powershell"><span </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Install required modules</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>pip install requests cryptography </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># ARM cross-compiler included in gcc_win/ folder</span> </span></span></code></pre></div><p><strong>macOS</strong>:</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-bash" data-lang="bash"><span style="color:#e6db74">"</span><span style="color:#66d9ef">$(</span>curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh<span style="color:#66d9ef">)</span><span style="color:#e6db74">"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Install Python 3</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>brew install python3 </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Install required modules</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>pip3 install requests cryptography </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># ARM toolchain included in gcc_mac/ folder</span> </span></span></code></pre></div><p><strong>Linux</strong>:</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-bash" data-lang="bash"><span style="display:flex;"><span>sudo apt-get install python3 python3-pip </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Install required modules</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>pip3 install requests cryptography </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Install ARM cross-compiler</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>sudo apt-get install gcc-arm-linux-gnueabihf </span></span></code></pre></div><h4 id="step-2-clone-repository"> <a href="#step-2-clone-repository" title="Step 2: Clone Repository">Step 2: Clone Repository</a> <a href="#step-2-clone-repository" style="display:flex;"><span>git clone https://github.com/dagnazty/DagShell.git </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>cd DagShell </span></span></code></pre></div><h4 id="step-3-build-firmware"> <a href="#step-3-build-firmware" title="Step 3: Build Firmware">Step 3: Build Firmware</a> <a href="#step-3-build-firmware" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Step 3: Build Firmware">#</a> </h4> <p><strong>Windows</strong>:</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-powershell" data-lang="powershell"><span style="display:flex;"><span>cd orbic_fw_c </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>python gen_pki.py <span style="color:#75715e"># Generate certificates</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>.\build.ps1 <span style="color:#75715e"># Compile firmware</span> </span></span></code></pre></div><p><strong>macOS/Linux</strong>:</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-bash" data-lang="bash"><span style="display:flex;"><span>cd orbic_fw_c </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>python3 gen_pki.py <span style="color:#75715e"># Generate certificates</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>./build.sh <span style="color:#75715e"># Compile firmware (auto-builds BearSSL)</span> </span></span></code></pre></div><p><strong>Output</strong>: <code>orbic_app</code> (static ARM binary) + DER certificate files</p> <p><strong>⚠️ Note for macOS</strong>: The <code>gcc_mac/</code> folder contains a custom ARM toolchain built with <strong>crosstool-ng</strong> targeting <strong>Linux kernel 3.2</strong> headers for compatibility with Orbic’s kernel 3.18. <em>Standard Homebrew ARM compilers target newer kernels and <strong>will not work</strong>.</em></p> <h4 id="step-4-enable-root-shell-on-orbic"> <a href="#step-4-enable-root-shell-on-orbic" title="Step 4: Enable Root Shell on Orbic">Step 4: Enable Root Shell on Orbic</a> <a href="#step-4-enable-root-shell-on-orbic" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Step 4: Enable Root Shell on Orbic">#</a> </h4> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-bash" data-lang="bash"><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Connect to Orbic WiFi</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Replace YOUR_ADMIN_PASSWORD with actual password</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>python enable_shell.py YOUR_ADMIN_PASSWORD </span></span></code></pre></div><p>This exploits Orbic web API to open root shell on <strong>port 24</strong>.</p> <h4 id="step-5-deploy-firmware"> <a href="#step-5-deploy-firmware" title="Step 5: Deploy Firmware">Step 5: Deploy Firmware</a> <a href="#step-5-deploy-firmware" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Step 5: Deploy Firmware">#</a> </h4> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-bash" data-lang="bash"><span style="display:flex;"><span>python deploy_base64.py </span></span></code></pre></div><p>This uploads and installs:</p> <ul> <li><code>orbic_app</code> to <code>/data/orbic_app</code></li> <li>Certificates to <code>/data/root.der</code> and <code>/data/server.der</code></li> <li>Boot script to <code>/etc/rc.d/dagshell_boot.sh</code></li> </ul> <p><strong>Persistence</strong>: Firmware auto-starts on reboot via init script</p> <h4 id="step-6-reboot-and-access"> <a href="#step-6-reboot-and-access" title="Step 6: Reboot and Access">Step 6: Reboot and Access</a> <a href="#step-6-reboot-and-access" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Step 6: Reboot and Access">#</a> </h4> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-bash" data-lang="bash"><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Reboot Orbic (power cycle or via SSH)</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>reboot </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># After reboot, access DagShell</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Browser: https://192.168.1.1:8443/</span> </span></span></code></pre></div><h3 id="security-certificate-warning"> <a href="#security-certificate-warning" title="Security Certificate Warning">Security Certificate Warning</a> <a href="#security-certificate-warning" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Security Certificate Warning">#</a> </h3> <p>When accessing DagShell, you’ll see a <strong>“Not Secure” or “Not Trusted”</strong> warning:</p> <p><strong>Desktop Browsers</strong>:</p> <ul> <li>Click <strong>“Advanced”</strong></li> <li>Click <strong>“Proceed to 192.168.1.1 (unsafe)”</strong></li> </ul> <p><strong>Mobile Browsers</strong>:</p> <ul> <li>Tap <strong>“Show Details”</strong></li> <li>Tap <strong>“visit this website”</strong></li> </ul> <p><strong>Why This Happens</strong>:</p> <ul> <li>Certificate is <strong>self-signed</strong> (not from trusted CA like Let’s Encrypt)</li> <li>Your device doesn’t have the root certificate in its trust store</li> <li><em>This is <strong>expected behavior</strong> for custom firmware, not a sign of compromise</em></li> </ul> <p><strong>Is Connection Actually Secure?</strong>:</p> <ul> <li><strong>YES</strong> - Connection IS encrypted with <strong>TLS 1.2+</strong></li> <li>Data cannot be intercepted</li> <li><em>The certificate is simply not “chain-trusted” to a public CA — the encryption is real</em></li> </ul> <p><strong>Optional</strong>: Install <code>root.der</code> in your device’s trust store to eliminate warning</p> <hr> <h2 id="raspberry-pi-companion-setup"> <a href="#raspberry-pi-companion-setup" title="Raspberry Pi Companion Setup">Raspberry Pi Companion Setup</a> <a href="#raspberry-pi-companion-setup" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Raspberry Pi Companion Setup">#</a> </h2> <p><strong>Optional but highly recommended</strong> for full functionality</p> <h3 id="hardware-setup"> <a href="#hardware-setup" title="Hardware Setup">Hardware Setup</a> <a href="#hardware-setup" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Hardware Setup">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>Step 1</strong>: Acquire Components</p> <ul> <li><strong>Raspberry Pi 3B+</strong> or newer ($35-55)</li> <li><strong>USB GPS dongle</strong> with U-Blox 7 chipset ($15-30)</li> <li><strong>MicroSD card</strong> 16GB+ for Pi OS ($10)</li> <li><strong>Power supply</strong> for Pi ($10)</li> </ul> <p><strong>Step 2</strong>: Install Raspberry Pi OS</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-bash" data-lang="bash"><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Download Raspberry Pi Imager</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># https://www.raspberrypi.org/software/</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Flash "Raspberry Pi OS Lite" to SD card</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Boot Pi and complete setup</span> </span></span></code></pre></div><p><strong>Step 3</strong>: Connect GPS Dongle</p> <ul> <li>Plug USB GPS into Pi</li> <li>Check detection: <code>lsusb</code> (should show GPS device)</li> <li>Verify serial port: <code>ls /dev/ttyUSB*</code> or <code>/dev/ttyACM*</code></li> </ul> <h3 id="software-installation"> <a href="#software-installation" title="Software Installation">Software Installation</a> <a href="#software-installation" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Software Installation">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>Step 1</strong>: Clone DagShell Repository on Pi</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-bash" data-lang="bash"><span style="display:flex;"><span>git clone https://github.com/dagnazty/DagShell.git </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>cd DagShell/pi_companion </span></span></code></pre></div><p><strong>Step 2</strong>: Install Dependencies</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-bash" data-lang="bash"><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Update package list</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>sudo apt-get update </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Install required packages</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>sudo apt-get install python3-serial python3-requests gpsd gpsd-clients </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Install Python modules</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>pip3 install pyserial gps3 bluetooth pybluez </span></span></code></pre></div><p><strong>Step 3</strong>: Configure GPS Daemon</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-bash" data-lang="bash"><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Edit gpsd config</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>sudo nano /etc/default/gpsd </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Set DEVICES line to your GPS serial port</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>DEVICES<span style="color:#f92672">=</span><span style="color:#e6db74">"/dev/ttyUSB0"</span> <span style="color:#75715e"># Adjust if different</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Restart gpsd</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>sudo systemctl restart gpsd </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Test GPS</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>cgps -s <span style="color:#75715e"># Should show satellite data after ~30 seconds</span> </span></span></code></pre></div><p><strong>Step 4</strong>: Configure Pi Companion</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-bash" data-lang="bash"><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Edit configuration file</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>nano pi_companion/config.py </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Set Orbic IP address</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>ORBIC_IP <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> <span style="color:#e6db74">"192.168.1.1"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>ORBIC_PORT <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> <span style="color:#e6db74">"8443"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Set GPS serial port</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>GPS_DEVICE <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> <span style="color:#e6db74">"/dev/ttyUSB0"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Save and exit</span> </span></span></code></pre></div><p><strong>Step 5</strong>: Install systemd Service</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-bash" data-lang="bash"><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Copy service file</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>sudo cp pi_companion.service /etc/systemd/system/ </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Reload systemd</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>sudo systemctl daemon-reload </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Enable auto-start</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>sudo systemctl enable pi-companion </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Start service</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>sudo systemctl start pi-companion </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Check status</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>sudo systemctl status pi-companion </span></span></code></pre></div><p><strong>Step 6</strong>: Verify Communication</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-bash" data-lang="bash"><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Check logs</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>journalctl -u pi-companion -f </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Should see GPS coordinates being sent to Orbic</span> </span></span></code></pre></div><h3 id="network-setup"> <a href="#network-setup" title="Network Setup">Network Setup</a> <a href="#network-setup" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Network Setup">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>Option A</strong>: WiFi Connection</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-bash" data-lang="bash"><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Pi connects to Orbic hotspot</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Edit wpa_supplicant config</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>sudo nano /etc/wpa_supplicant/wpa_supplicant.conf </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Add network</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>network<span style="color:#f92672">={</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> ssid<span style="color:#f92672">=</span><span style="color:#e6db74">"Orbic_SSID"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> psk<span style="color:#f92672">=</span><span style="color:#e6db74">"PASSWORD"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#f92672">}</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Restart WiFi</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>sudo systemctl restart networking </span></span></code></pre></div><p><strong>Option B</strong>: USB Ethernet (Recommended)</p> <ul> <li>Connect Pi to Orbic via <strong>USB cable</strong> (Pi USB port to Orbic USB port)</li> <li>Enable <strong>USB gadget mode</strong> on Pi</li> <li>Pi acts as <strong>USB Ethernet device</strong></li> <li><em>More reliable than WiFi for continuous data transfer</em></li> <li>Pi accesses internet through Orbic</li> </ul> <p><strong>USB Gadget Config</strong>:</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-bash" data-lang="bash"><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Edit config.txt</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>sudo nano /boot/config.txt </span></span><span device</li> <li>Randomize MAC address</li> <li>Monitor IMSI catcher detector continuously</li> <li>Use AdBlock for all connected devices</li> <li>Log suspicious cellular activity</li> </ol> <p><strong>Profile</strong>: Researcher analyzing IoT device security</p> <p><strong>DagShell Tools Used</strong>:</p> <ul> <li><strong>ARP scanner</strong> with OUI lookup to identify IoT devices</li> <li>Port scan for vulnerable services</li> <li>BLE scan for Bluetooth IoT devices</li> <li>DNS sniff to analyze “phone home” behavior</li> <li>Document vulnerabilities</li> </ol> <p><strong>RayHunter Integration</strong>: Detect if IoT devices have cellular connectivity and monitor for anomalies</p> <hr> <h2 id="legal-and-ethical-considerations"> <a href="#legal-and-ethical-considerations" title="Legal and Ethical Considerations">Legal and Ethical Considerations</a> <a href="#legal-and-ethical-considerations" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Legal and Ethical Considerations">#</a> </h2> <h3 id="legal-framework"> <a href="#legal-framework" title="Legal Framework">Legal Framework</a> <a href="#legal-framework" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Legal Framework">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>Tools Like DagShell Exist in Legal Gray Areas</strong>:</p> <p><strong>Legal Uses</strong>: ✅ <strong>Your own networks</strong> - Test your own devices and networks ✅ <strong>Authorized testing</strong> - Penetration testing with written permission ✅ <strong>Educational purposes</strong> - Learning in isolated lab environments ✅ <strong>Privacy protection</strong> - TTL fix, MAC spoofing on your device ✅ <strong>Security research</strong> - Responsible disclosure vulnerability research</p> <p><strong>Illegal Uses</strong>: ❌ <strong>Unauthorized access</strong> - Attacking networks you don’t own (CFAA violation) ❌ <strong>Deauth attacks</strong> on other networks (FCC violation + possible felony) ❌ <strong>Evil Twin</strong> attacks against public (wire fraud, identity theft) ❌ <strong>DNS sniffing</strong> of others without consent (wiretapping) ❌ <strong>Bypassing security</strong> to commit fraud</p> <h3 id="laws-to-be-aware-of"> <a href="#laws-to-be-aware-of" title="Laws to Be Aware Of">Laws to Be Aware Of</a> <a href="#laws-to-be-aware-of" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Laws to Be Aware Of">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>United States</strong>:</p> <ul> <li><strong>Computer Fraud and Abuse Act (CFAA)</strong> - Unauthorized access to computers/networks</li> <li><strong>Wiretap Act</strong> - Intercepting electronic communications</li> <li><strong>FCC Regulations</strong> - Radio frequency interference (deauth attacks)</li> </ul> <p><strong>United Kingdom</strong>:</p> <ul> <li><strong>Computer Misuse Act 1990</strong> - Similar to CFAA</li> <li><strong>Regulation of Investigatory Powers Act (RIPA)</strong> - Interception restrictions</li> </ul> <p><strong>European Union</strong>:</p> <ul> <li><strong>GDPR</strong> - Data protection (BLE/WiFi scanning of identifiable data)</li> <li><em>Local regulations vary by country and enforcement differs significantly</em></li> </ul> <h3 id="ethical-guidelines"> <a href="#ethical-guidelines" title="Ethical Guidelines">Ethical Guidelines</a> <a href="#ethical-guidelines" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Ethical Guidelines">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>DO</strong>:</p> <ul> <li>✅ Use on <strong>your own devices</strong> and networks</li> <li>✅ Obtain <strong>written permission</strong> before testing others’ systems</li> <li>✅ <strong>Responsible disclosure</strong> if you find vulnerabilities</li> <li>✅ <strong>Educational use</strong> in controlled environments</li> <li>✅ <strong>Privacy protection</strong> for yourself</li> </ul> <p><strong>DON’T</strong>:</p> <ul> <li>❌ Attack networks you don’t own</li> <li>❌ Harvest credentials from strangers</li> <li>❌ Interfere with critical infrastructure</li> <li>❌ Use tools maliciously</li> <li>❌ Distribute captured data</li> </ul> <h3 id="responsible-use-statement"> <a href="#responsible-use-statement" title="Responsible Use Statement">Responsible Use Statement</a> <a href="#responsible-use-statement" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Responsible Use Statement">#</a> </h3> <p>DagShell is a <strong>security research and privacy tool</strong>. Its capabilities are similar to professional penetration testing tools like <strong>Kali Linux</strong>, <strong>WiFi Pineapple</strong>, or <strong>HackRF</strong>.</p> <p><strong>Intent Matters</strong>: The same tools used by <strong>malicious hackers</strong> are used by <strong>security professionals</strong>. The difference is:</p> <ul> <li><strong>Authorization</strong> (permission to test)</li> <li><strong>Intent</strong> (improve security vs. cause harm)</li> <li><strong>Disclosure</strong> (report findings vs. exploit them)</li> </ul> <p>Use DagShell <strong>responsibly</strong> and <strong>ethically</strong>. <em>If you’re unsure whether something is legal, stop and consult a lawyer before proceeding.</em></p> <hr> <h2 id="troubleshooting"> <a href="#troubleshooting" title="Troubleshooting">Troubleshooting</a> <a href="#troubleshooting" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Troubleshooting">#</a> </h2> <h3 id="installation-issues"> <a href="#installation-issues" title="Installation Issues">Installation Issues</a> <a href="#installation-issues" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Installation Issues">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>Problem</strong>: <code>enable_shell.py</code> fails to connect</p> <p><strong>Solutions</strong>:</p> <ul> <li>Verify you’re connected to <strong>Orbic WiFi</strong></li> <li>Check IP address is <strong>192.168.1.1</strong> (default)</li> <li>Try admin password again (case-sensitive)</li> <li>Check if port 80 is accessible: <code>telnet 192.168.1.1 80</code></li> </ul> <p><strong>Problem</strong>: Firmware upload fails</p> <p><strong>Solutions</strong>:</p> <ul> <li>Ensure root shell enabled first (<code>enable_shell.py</code> succeeded)</li> <li>Check network stability (use wired connection if possible)</li> <li>Try <code>deploy_net.py</code> instead of <code>deploy_base64.py</code></li> <li>Verify <code>/data</code> partition has space: <code>df -h /data</code></li> </ul> <p><strong>Problem</strong>: Certificate warning won’t go away</p> <p><strong>Solutions</strong>:</p> <ul> <li><em>This is <strong>normal</strong> for self-signed certificates — the connection is still encrypted</em></li> <li>Click “Advanced” -> “Proceed anyway” each time</li> <li><strong>Optional</strong>: Install <code>root.der</code> in device trust store (advanced)</li> </ul> <h3 id="runtime-issues"> <a href="#runtime-issues" title="Runtime Issues">Runtime Issues</a> <a href="#runtime-issues" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Runtime Issues">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>Problem</strong>: DagShell not starting on boot</p> <p><strong>Solutions</strong>:</p> <ul> <li>Check boot script: <code>ls -la /etc/rc.d/dagshell_boot.sh</code></li> <li>Verify executable: <code>chmod +x /etc/rc.d/dagshell_boot.sh</code></li> <li>Check logs: <code>logread | grep dagshell</code></li> <li>Manually start: <code>/data/orbic_app &</code></li> </ul> <p><strong>Problem</strong>: GPS not working</p> <p><strong>Solutions</strong>:</p> <ul> <li>Verify <strong>Pi companion</strong> is running: <code>systemctl status pi-companion</code></li> <li>Check GPS has satellite lock: <code>cgps -s</code> (on Pi)</li> <li>Ensure GPS dongle connected: <code>lsusb</code></li> <li><em>Wait 5-10 minutes for cold start GPS fix — GPS fix takes longer outdoors on first boot</em></li> </ul> <p><strong>Problem</strong>: Wardriving not logging</p> <p><strong>Solutions</strong>:</p> <ul> <li>Ensure GPS has fix before starting</li> <li>Check <code>/data</code> has write permissions</li> <li>Verify CSV file being created: <code>ls -la /data/wardrive_log.csv</code></li> <li>Check WiFi interface is up: <code>ifconfig wlan0</code></li> </ul> <p><strong>Problem</strong>: Pi companion not communicating with Orbic</p> <p><strong>Solutions</strong>:</p> <ul> <li>Verify network connectivity: <code>ping 192.168.1.1</code></li> <li>Check config file has correct IP</li> <li>Ensure port 8443 is open: <code>telnet 192.168.1.1 8443</code></li> <li>Check Pi companion logs: <code>journalctl -u pi-companion</code></li> </ul> <h3 id="performance-issues"> <a href="#performance-issues" title="Performance Issues">Performance Issues</a> <a href="#performance-issues" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Performance Issues">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>Problem</strong>: Web interface slow/laggy</p> <p><strong>Solutions</strong>:</p> <ul> <li>Clear browser cache</li> <li>Disable browser extensions (ad blockers interfere)</li> <li>Use <strong>wired</strong> connection to Orbic instead of WiFi</li> <li>Restart DagShell: <code>killall orbic_app && /data/orbic_app &</code></li> </ul> <p><strong>Problem</strong>: Wardriving scans take too long</p> <p><strong>Solutions</strong>:</p> <ul> <li>Increase scan interval in settings (trade frequency for performance)</li> <li>Reduce number of channels scanned</li> <li><em>Weak GPS fix slows scans — the system waits for valid coordinates before logging</em></li> </ul> <hr> <h2 id="advanced-configuration"> <a href="#advanced-configuration" title="Advanced Configuration">Advanced Configuration</a> <a href="#advanced-configuration" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Advanced Configuration">#</a> </h2> <h3 id="customizing-dagshell"> <a href="#customizing-dagshell" title="Customizing DagShell">Customizing DagShell</a> <a href="#customizing-dagshell" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Customizing DagShell">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>Modifying Source Code</strong>:</p> <ul> <li>Edit files in <code>orbic_fw_c/</code> directory</li> <li>Rebuild with <code>build.sh</code> or <code>build.ps1</code></li> <li>Redeploy firmware</li> </ul> <p><strong>Adding Custom Pages</strong>:</p> <ul> <li>DagShell uses embedded HTML in C++ code</li> <li>Edit <code>main.cpp</code> to add new routes</li> <li>Recompile and deploy</li> </ul> <p><strong>Custom Certificate</strong>:</p> <ul> <li>Generate your own PKI with <code>gen_pki.py</code></li> <li>Or use <strong>OpenSSL</strong> to create certificates</li> <li>Replace <code>root.der</code> and <code>server.der</code></li> </ul> <h3 id="integrating-with-other-tools"> <a href="#integrating-with-other-tools" title="Integrating with Other Tools">Integrating with Other Tools</a> <a href="#integrating-with-other-tools" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Integrating with Other Tools">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>Exporting Data to Wireshark</strong>:</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-bash" data-lang="bash"><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Capture pcap file</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>tcpdump -i wlan0 -w /data/capture.pcap </span></span><span style="color:#75715e"># Open in Wireshark on PC</span> </span></span></code></pre></div><p><strong>Importing GPS Tracks to QGIS</strong>:</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-bash" data-lang="bash"><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Export GPX format</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>python gps_to_gpx.py /data/gps_coords.txt </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Import to QGIS as vector layer</span> </span></span></code></pre></div><p><strong>Syncing with External Database</strong>:</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-python" data-lang="python"><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Custom script to upload wardrive data to MySQL</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#f92672">import</span> mysql.connector </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#f92672">import</span> csv </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>conn <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> mysql<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>connector<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>connect( </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> host<span style="color:#f92672">=</span><span style="color:#e6db74">"your_server"</span>, </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> user<span style="color:#f92672">=</span><span style="color:#e6db74">"username"</span>, </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> password<span style="color:#f92672">=</span><span style="color:#e6db74">"password"</span>, </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> database<span style="color:#f92672">=</span><span style="color:#e6db74">"wardriving"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>) </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#66d9ef">with</span> open(<span style="color:#e6db74">'/data/wardrive_log.csv'</span>) <span style="color:#66d9ef">as</span> f: </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> reader <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> csv<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>DictReader(f) </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">for</span> row <span style="color:#f92672">in</span> reader: </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> cursor<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>execute(<span style="color:#e6db74">"INSERT INTO networks (ssid, bssid, lat, lon) VALUES (</span><span style="color:#e6db74">%s</span><span style="color:#e6db74">, </span><span style="color:#e6db74">%s</span><span style="color:#e6db74">, </span><span style="color:#e6db74">%s</span><span style="color:#e6db74">, </span><span style="color:#e6db74">%s</span><span style="color:#e6db74">)"</span>, </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> (row[<span style="color:#e6db74">'SSID'</span>], row[<span style="color:#e6db74">'MAC'</span>], row[<span style="color:#e6db74">'Latitude'</span>], row[<span style="color:#e6db74">'Longitude'</span>])) </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>conn<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>commit() </span></span></code></pre></div><h3 id="performance-tuning"> <a href="#performance-tuning" title="Performance Tuning">Performance Tuning</a> <a href="#performance-tuning" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Performance Tuning">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>Optimizing Scan Speed</strong>:</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-bash" data-lang="bash"><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Edit wardrive scan interval</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># In DagShell web UI, change from 5s to 3s for faster scanning</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Trade-off: Higher CPU usage, potential missed networks</span> </span></span></code></pre></div><p><strong>Battery Optimization</strong> (if using battery pack):</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-bash" data-lang="bash"><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Reduce screen brightness on Orbic</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Disable unused features (BT, GPS when not needed)</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Use sleep mode between wardrive sessions</span> </span></span></code></pre></div><hr> <h2 id="comparison-to-alternatives"> <a href="#comparison-to-alternatives" title="Comparison to Alternatives">Comparison to Alternatives</a> <a href="#comparison-to-alternatives" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Comparison to Alternatives">#</a> </h2> <h3 id="dagshell-vs-wifi-pineapple"> <a href="#dagshell-vs-wifi-pineapple" title="DagShell vs. WiFi Pineapple">DagShell vs. WiFi Pineapple</a> <a href="#dagshell-vs-wifi-pineapple" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="DagShell vs. WiFi Pineapple">#</a> </h3> <table> <thead> <tr> <th>Feature</th> <th>DagShell (Orbic RCL400)</th> <th>WiFi Pineapple</th> </tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr> <td><strong>Price</strong></td> <td>$60-80 + device</td> <td>$99-299</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Cellular</strong></td> <td>✅ 4G LTE built-in</td> <td>❌ No (WiFi only)</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>GPS</strong></td> <td>✅ Via Pi companion</td> <td>⚠️ Via USB dongle</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Evil Twin</strong></td> <td>✅ Yes</td> <td>✅ Yes</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Captive Portal</strong></td> <td>✅ Yes</td> <td>✅ Yes</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Deauth</strong></td> <td>✅ Yes (via Pi)</td> <td>✅ Yes</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Wardriving</strong></td> <td>✅ Yes</td> <td>✅ Yes</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>IMSI Detection</strong></td> <td>✅ Basic</td> <td>❌ No</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Portability</strong></td> <td>✅ Highly portable</td> <td>⚠️ Moderate</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Battery</strong></td> <td>✅ Built-in</td> <td>⚠️ External required</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Learning Curve</strong></td> <td>Moderate</td> <td>Low (polished UI)</td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <p><strong>Winner</strong>: DagShell for <strong>mobile cellular security</strong>, Pineapple for <strong>WiFi-specific</strong> testing with easy UI</p> <h3 id="dagshell-vs-kali-linux-on-pi"> <a href="#dagshell-vs-kali-linux-on-pi" title="DagShell vs. Kali Linux on Pi">DagShell vs. Kali Linux on Pi</a> <a href="#dagshell-vs-kali-linux-on-pi" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="DagShell vs. Kali Linux on Pi">#</a> </h3> <table> <thead> <tr> <th>Feature</th> <th>DagShell</th> <th>Kali on Pi</th> </tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr> <td><strong>Setup Time</strong></td> <td>15 minutes</td> <td>2-3 hours</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Cellular LTE</strong></td> <td>✅ Built-in</td> <td>❌ Requires USB modem</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Web Interface</strong></td> <td>✅ Yes</td> <td>⚠️ Limited (requires VNC/SSH)</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Tool Count</strong></td> <td>~15</td> <td>600+</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Specialization</strong></td> <td>Mobile security</td> <td>General pentesting</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Portability</strong></td> <td>✅ Pocket-sized</td> <td>⚠️ Requires screen/power</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Battery Life</strong></td> <td>8-10 hours</td> <td>2-4 hours</td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <p><strong>Winner</strong>: DagShell for <strong>mobile-specific</strong> tasks, Kali for <strong>comprehensive</strong> toolkit</p> <h3 id="dagshell-vs-stock-orbic-firmware"> <a href="#dagshell-vs-stock-orbic-firmware" title="DagShell vs. Stock Orbic Firmware">DagShell vs. Stock Orbic Firmware</a> <a href="#dagshell-vs-stock-orbic-firmware" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="DagShell vs. Stock Orbic Firmware">#</a> </h3> <table> <thead> <tr> <th>Feature</th> <th>DagShell</th> <th>Stock Firmware</th> </tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr> <td><strong>Security Tools</strong></td> <td>✅ Extensive</td> <td>❌ None</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Privacy Features</strong></td> <td>✅ Yes</td> <td>❌ Minimal</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Customization</strong></td> <td>✅ Fully customizable</td> <td>❌ Locked</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Wardriving</strong></td> <td>✅ Yes</td> <td>❌ No</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>GPS</strong></td> <td>✅ Yes (via Pi)</td> <td>❌ No</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Open Source</strong></td> <td>✅ Yes</td> <td>❌ Proprietary</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Support</strong></td> <td>⚠️ Community</td> <td>✅ Official</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Stability</strong></td> <td>⚠️ Beta</td> <td>✅ Production</td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <p><strong>Winner</strong>: DagShell for <strong>researchers</strong>, Stock for <strong>casual users</strong></p> <hr> <h2 id="conclusion-the-ultimate-mobile-lab"> <a href="#conclusion-the-ultimate-mobile-lab" title="Conclusion: The Ultimate Mobile Lab">Conclusion: The Ultimate Mobile Lab</a> <a href="#conclusion-the-ultimate-mobile-lab" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Conclusion: The Ultimate Mobile Lab">#</a> </h2> <p><strong>DagShell</strong> transforms the humble <strong>Orbic RCL400 hotspot</strong> into a <strong>powerful mobile security laboratory</strong> combining:</p> <p>✅ <strong>Privacy protection</strong> (TTL masking, MAC spoofing, AdBlock) ✅ <strong>Network monitoring</strong> (connections, DNS, routing) ✅ <strong>Hacking tools</strong> (IMSI detection, port scanning, ARP discovery) ✅ <strong>Attack capabilities</strong> (Evil Twin, captive portal, deauth) ✅ <strong>GPS wardriving</strong> with Wigle integration ✅ <strong>Raspberry Pi expansion</strong> (BLE, WiFi, GPS) ✅ <strong>Portable and battery-powered</strong> ✅ <strong>Open source and customizable</strong></p> <h3 id="when-combined-with-rayhunter"> <a href="#when-combined-with-rayhunter" title="When Combined with RayHunter">When Combined with RayHunter</a> <a href="#when-combined-with-rayhunter" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="When Combined with RayHunter">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>RayHunter + DagShell</strong> = <strong>Complete mobile security platform</strong>:</p> <ul> <li><strong>RayHunter</strong> provides advanced IMSI catcher detection</li> <li><strong>DagShell</strong> provides offensive and privacy tools</li> <li>Both run <strong>simultaneously</strong> on same device</li> <li><strong>Complementary capabilities</strong> with no overlap</li> <li><strong>Single portable package</strong> for all mobile security needs</li> </ul> <h3 id="get-started-today"> <a href="#get-started-today" title="Get Started Today">Get Started Today</a> <a href="#get-started-today" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Get Started Today">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>Option 1: Pre-Flashed</strong> (Easiest)</p> <ul> <li><strong>STS Collective</strong>: <span itemscope itemtype="https://schema.org/WebPage"> <meta itemprop="name" content="Orbic RCL400 with RayHunter + DagShell"> <meta itemprop="url" content="https://stscollective.com/products/orbic-rcl400-rayhunter-dagshell-hotspot"> <a href="https://stscollective.com/products/orbic-rcl400-rayhunter-dagshell-hotspot" rel="noopener external" target="_blank" >Orbic RCL400 with RayHunter + DagShell</a> </span> </li> <li>Arrive <strong>ready to use</strong></li> <li>No installation hassle</li> <li>Professionally configured</li> </ul> <p><strong>Option 2: DIY Installation</strong></p> <ul> <li>Follow this guide</li> <li><strong>Free</strong> if you own Orbic RCL400</li> <li>Learn the installation process</li> <li>Customize to your needs</li> </ul> <h3 id="related-reading"> <a href="#related-reading" title="Related Reading">Related Reading</a> <a href="#related-reading" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Related Reading">#</a> </h3> <ul> <li><strong> <span itemscope itemtype="https://schema.org/WebPage"> <meta itemprop="name" content="How to Flash RayHunter Devices: Complete Guide"> <meta itemprop="url" content="/articles/how-to-flash-rayhunter-devices-complete-guide/"> <a href="/articles/how-to-flash-rayhunter-devices-complete-guide/" rel="follow me" >How to Flash RayHunter Devices: Complete Guide</a> </span> </strong> - Install RayHunter first</li> <li><strong> <span itemscope itemtype="https://schema.org/WebPage"> <meta itemprop="name" content="RayHunter Device Comparison: Complete Review"> <meta itemprop="url" content="/articles/rayhunter-device-comparison-2026-complete-review/"> <a href="/articles/rayhunter-device-comparison-2026-complete-review/" rel="follow me" >RayHunter Device Comparison: Complete Review</a> </span> </strong> - Choose your platform</li> <li><strong> <span itemscope itemtype="https://schema.org/WebPage"> <meta itemprop="name" content="Flock-You Detection Project: Counter-Surveillance Hardware Guide"> <meta itemprop="url" content="/articles/flock-you-detection-project-counter-surveillance-hardware-guide-2026/"> <a href="/articles/flock-you-detection-project-counter-surveillance-hardware-guide-2026/" rel="follow me" >Flock-You Detection Project: Counter-Surveillance Hardware Guide</a> </span> </strong> - More detection tools</li> </ul> <h3 id="final-thoughts"> <a href="#final-thoughts" title="Final Thoughts">Final Thoughts</a> <a href="#final-thoughts" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Final Thoughts">#</a> </h3> <p>Whether you’re a <strong>security researcher</strong>, <strong>penetration tester</strong>, <strong>privacy advocate</strong>, or <strong>network administrator</strong>, DagShell provides a <strong>portable, powerful, and affordable</strong> platform for mobile security work.</p> <p><strong>Disclaimer</strong>: Use responsibly. Only test networks and devices you own or have explicit written permission to assess. Stay legal, stay ethical, and happy hacking! 🚀</p> <hr> <h2 id="references"> <a href="#references" title="References">References</a> <a href="#references" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="References">#</a> </h2> <ol> <li> <span itemscope itemtype="https://schema.org/WebPage"> <meta itemprop="name" content="DagShell GitHub Repository"> <meta itemprop="url" content="https://github.com/dagnazty/DagShell"> <a href="https://github.com/dagnazty/DagShell" rel="noopener external" target="_blank" >DagShell GitHub Repository</a> </span> </li> <li> <span itemscope itemtype="https://schema.org/WebPage"> <meta itemprop="name" content="DagShell Documentation"> <meta itemprop="url" content="https://dagnazty.github.io/DagShell/"> <a href="https://dagnazty.github.io/DagShell/" rel="noopener external" target="_blank" >DagShell Documentation</a> </span> </li> <li> <span itemscope itemtype="https://schema.org/WebPage"> <meta itemprop="name" content="STS Collective - Pre-Flashed Devices"> <meta itemprop="url" content="https://stscollective.com/products/orbic-rcl400-rayhunter-dagshell-hotspot"> <a href="https://stscollective.com/products/orbic-rcl400-rayhunter-dagshell-hotspot" rel="noopener external" target="_blank" >STS Collective - Pre-Flashed Devices</a> </span> </li> <li> <span itemscope itemtype="https://schema.org/WebPage"> <meta itemprop="name" content="WiGLE - WiFi Mapping Project"> <meta itemprop="url" content="https://wigle.net/"> <a href="https://wigle.net/" rel="noopener external" target="_blank" >WiGLE - WiFi Mapping Project</a> </span> </li> <li> <span itemscope itemtype="https://schema.org/WebPage"> <meta itemprop="name" content="Computer Fraud and Abuse Act (CFAA)"> <meta itemprop="url" content="https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/18/1030"> <a href="https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/18/1030" rel="noopener external" target="_blank" >Computer Fraud and Abuse Act (CFAA)</a> </span> </li> <li> <span itemscope itemtype="https://schema.org/WebPage"> <meta itemprop="name" content="Raspberry Pi Official Documentation"> <meta itemprop="url" content="https://www.raspberrypi.org/documentation/"> <a href="https://www.raspberrypi.org/documentation/" rel="noopener external" target="_blank" >Raspberry Pi Official Documentation</a> </span> </li> <li> <span itemscope itemtype="https://schema.org/WebPage"> <meta itemprop="name" content="U-Blox GPS Module Documentation"> <meta itemprop="url" content="https://www.u-blox.com/"> <a href="https://www.u-blox.com/" rel="noopener external" target="_blank" >U-Blox GPS Module Documentation</a> </span> </li> <li> <span itemscope itemtype="https://schema.org/WebPage"> <meta itemprop="name" content="OUI Database - IEEE Standards"> <meta itemprop="url" content="https://standards.ieee.org/products-programs/regauth/"> <a href="https://standards.ieee.org/products-programs/regauth/" rel="noopener external" target="_blank" >OUI Database - IEEE Standards</a> </span> </li> <li> <span itemscope itemtype="https://schema.org/WebPage"> <meta itemprop="name" content="iptables Tutorial"> <meta itemprop="url" content="https://www.netfilter.org/documentation/"> <a href="https://www.netfilter.org/documentation/" rel="noopener external" target="_blank" >iptables Tutorial</a> </span> </li> <li> <span itemscope itemtype="https://schema.org/WebPage"> <meta itemprop="name" content="OpenSSL Documentation"> <meta itemprop="url" content="https://www.openssl.org/docs/"> <a href="https://www.openssl.org/docs/" rel="noopener external" target="_blank" >OpenSSL Documentation</a> </span> </li> </ol> Read More at https://simeononsecurity.ch/

item.title: Ansible vs Puppet vs Chef 2026: Complete Configuration Management Comparison - Features, Performance & Best Use Cases
item.category: "DevOps" , "Infrastructure Automation" , "Configuration Management" , "IT Operations" , "Cloud Computing" , "System Administration" , "Enterprise IT" , "Software Development" , "IT Infrastructure" , "Automation Tools"
item.description: Comprehensive 2026 comparison of Ansible, Puppet, and Chef configuration management tools. Detailed analysis of architecture, features, learning curve, performance, pricing, and use cases to help you choose the best automation tool for infrastructure management.
item.content:encoded: <h2 id="ansible-vs-puppet-vs-chef-2026-complete-configuration-management-comparison"> <a href="#ansible-vs-puppet-vs-chef-2026-complete-configuration-management-comparison" title="Ansible vs Puppet vs Chef 2026: Complete Configuration Management Comparison">Ansible vs Puppet vs Chef 2026: Complete Configuration Management Comparison</a> <a href="#ansible-vs-puppet-vs-chef-2026-complete-configuration-management-comparison" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Ansible vs Puppet vs Chef 2026: Complete Configuration Management Comparison">#</a> </h2> <p>In 2026, <strong>configuration management</strong> remains critical for managing modern infrastructure at scale, with <strong>Ansible</strong>, <strong>Puppet</strong>, and <strong>Chef</strong> dominating the enterprise market. These tools enable <strong>Infrastructure as Code (IaC)</strong>, allowing teams to automate server configuration, application deployment, and infrastructure orchestration across thousands of systems.</p> <p>With <strong>87% of enterprises</strong> using at least one configuration management tool and the average organization managing <strong>500+ servers</strong>, choosing the right tooling significantly impacts operational efficiency, deployment velocity, and infrastructure reliability.</p> <p>This comprehensive guide compares <strong>Ansible</strong>, <strong>Puppet</strong>, and <strong>Chef</strong> across architecture, features, performance, learning curve, pricing, and real-world use cases to help you make an informed decision for your infrastructure automation needs.</p> <h3 id="the-state-of-configuration-management-in-2026"> <a href="#the-state-of-configuration-management-in-2026" title="The State of Configuration Management in 2026">The State of Configuration Management in 2026</a> <a href="#the-state-of-configuration-management-in-2026" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="The State of Configuration Management in 2026">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>Market Overview</strong>:</p> <ul> <li><strong>Ansible</strong>: 42% market share (Red Hat/IBM, most popular)</li> <li><strong>Puppet</strong>: 28% market share (established enterprise base)</li> <li><strong>Chef</strong>: 18% market share (DevOps-focused organizations)</li> <li><strong>Other tools</strong>: 12% (SaltStack, CFEngine, custom solutions)</li> </ul> <p><strong>Key Trends</strong>:</p> <ul> <li>Shift toward <strong>agentless architecture</strong> (Ansible’s advantage)</li> <li><strong>Cloud-native integration</strong> with AWS, Azure, GCP</li> <li><strong>Kubernetes and container orchestration</strong> becoming primary use case</li> <li><strong>GitOps workflows</strong> for infrastructure management</li> <li><strong>Policy as Code</strong> for compliance automation</li> </ul> <h2 id="quick-comparison-at-a-glance"> <a href="#quick-comparison-at-a-glance" title="Quick Comparison: At a Glance">Quick Comparison: At a Glance</a> <a href="#quick-comparison-at-a-glance" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Quick Comparison: At a Glance">#</a> </h2> <table> <thead> <tr> <th>Feature</th> <th>Ansible</th> <th>Puppet</th> <th>Chef</th> </tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr> <td><strong>Architecture</strong></td> <td>Agentless (SSH/WinRM)</td> <td>Agent-based (pull model)</td> <td>Agent-based (pull model)</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Language</strong></td> <td>YAML (procedural)</td> <td>Puppet DSL (declarative)</td> <td>Ruby DSL (procedural)</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Learning Curve</strong></td> <td>Easy (hours to days)</td> <td>Moderate (days to weeks)</td> <td>Steep (weeks to months)</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Initial Setup</strong></td> <td>Minimal (control node only)</td> <td>Complex (master + agents)</td> <td>Complex (server + nodes)</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Configuration Style</strong></td> <td>Procedural (order matters)</td> <td>Declarative (state-based)</td> <td>Procedural (recipes)</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Scalability</strong></td> <td>Good (up to 5,000+ nodes)</td> <td>Excellent (10,000+ nodes)</td> <td>Excellent (10,000+ nodes)</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Performance</strong></td> <td>Fast (SSH overhead)</td> <td>Fast (efficient pull)</td> <td>Fast (efficient pull)</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Cloud Integration</strong></td> <td>Excellent (native modules)</td> <td>Good (modules available)</td> <td>Good (resources available)</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Community</strong></td> <td>Largest (200k+ modules)</td> <td>Large (6,000+ modules)</td> <td>Large (5,000+ cookbooks)</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Enterprise Support</strong></td> <td>Red Hat (Ansible Automation Platform)</td> <td>Perforce (Puppet Enterprise)</td> <td>Progress (Chef Automate)</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Pricing (Open Source)</strong></td> <td>Free</td> <td>Free</td> <td>Free</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Pricing (Enterprise)</strong></td> <td>$5,000-$10,000/100 nodes/year</td> <td>$6,000-$15,000/100 nodes/year</td> <td>$7,000-$13,500/100 nodes/year</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Best For</strong></td> <td>General automation, cloud, agentless</td> <td>Large enterprises, compliance</td> <td>DevOps teams, infrastructure as code</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Primary Weakness</strong></td> <td>SSH overhead at scale</td> <td>Agent complexity</td> <td>Steep learning curve</td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <h2 id="architecture-comparison"> <a href="#architecture-comparison" title="Architecture Comparison">Architecture Comparison</a> <a href="#architecture-comparison" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Architecture Comparison">#</a> </h2> <h3 id="ansible-agentless-push-model"> <a href="#ansible-agentless-push-model" title="Ansible: Agentless Push Model">Ansible: Agentless Push Model</a> <a href="#ansible-agentless-push-model" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Ansible: Agentless Push Model">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>Architecture</strong>:</p> <ul> <li><strong>Control Node</strong>: Where Ansible runs (Linux/macOS only)</li> <li><strong>Managed Nodes</strong>: Target systems (Linux, Windows, network devices)</li> <li><strong>Communication</strong>: SSH (Linux), WinRM (Windows), APIs (network/cloud)</li> <li><strong>No agents required</strong>: Uses existing SSH infrastructure</li> </ul> <p><strong>How It Works</strong>:</p> <ol> <li>Admin runs playbook from control node</li> <li>Ansible connects to managed nodes via SSH/WinRM</li> <li>Python modules executed on target systems</li> <li>Results returned to control node</li> <li>Connection closed (stateless)</li> </ol> <p><strong>Advantages</strong>: ✅ <strong>Quick setup</strong>: No agent deployment required ✅ <strong>Lower maintenance</strong>: No agent updates or monitoring ✅ <strong>Simpler architecture</strong>: Fewer moving parts ✅ <strong>Immediate execution</strong>: Push-based, runs when triggered</p> <p><strong>Disadvantages</strong>: ⚠️ <strong>SSH overhead</strong>: Connection establishment takes time at scale ⚠️ <strong>No continuous enforcement</strong>: Doesn’t monitor/correct drift automatically ⚠️ <strong>Control node dependency</strong>: Single point of failure (mitigated by clustering) ⚠️ <strong>Network connectivity</strong>: Requires SSH access to all nodes</p> <h3 id="puppet-agent-based-pull-model"> <a href="#puppet-agent-based-pull-model" title="Puppet: Agent-Based Pull Model">Puppet: Agent-Based Pull Model</a> <a href="#puppet-agent-based-pull-model" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Puppet: Agent-Based Pull Model">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>Architecture</strong>:</p> <ul> <li><strong>Puppet Server (Master)</strong>: Central configuration authority</li> <li><strong>Puppet Agents</strong>: Installed on every managed node</li> <li><strong>PuppetDB</strong>: Stores facts, catalogs, and reports</li> <li><strong>Certificate-based authentication</strong>: Secure communication</li> </ul> <p><strong>How It Works</strong>:</p> <ol> <li>Agents check in with server every 30 minutes (configurable)</li> <li>Agent sends system facts to server</li> <li>Server compiles catalog (desired state) for that agent</li> <li>Agent applies catalog to bring system into compliance</li> <li>Agent reports results back to server</li> </ol> <p><strong>Advantages</strong>: ✅ <strong>Continuous enforcement</strong>: Agents constantly maintain desired state ✅ <strong>Automatic drift correction</strong>: Detects and fixes configuration drift ✅ <strong>Scalable</strong>: Handles 10,000+ nodes efficiently ✅ <strong>Reporting</strong>: Detailed compliance and change reporting</p> <p><strong>Disadvantages</strong>: ⚠️ <strong>Agent installation</strong>: Requires agent on every system ⚠️ <strong>Agent maintenance</strong>: Agents need updates and monitoring ⚠️ <strong>Complex setup</strong>: Master/agent infrastructure requires planning ⚠️ <strong>Resource usage</strong>: Agents consume CPU/memory on managed nodes</p> <h3 id="chef-agent-based-pull-model"> <a href="#chef-agent-based-pull-model" title="Chef: Agent-Based Pull Model">Chef: Agent-Based Pull Model</a> <a href="#chef-agent-based-pull-model" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Chef: Agent-Based Pull Model">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>Architecture</strong>:</p> <ul> <li><strong>Chef Server</strong>: Central configuration repository</li> <li><strong>Chef Clients (Nodes)</strong>: Agents on managed systems</li> <li><strong>Workstation</strong>: Where admins develop cookbooks</li> <li><strong>Chef Automate</strong>: Enterprise platform for orchestration</li> </ul> <p><strong>How It Works</strong>:</p> <ol> <li>Admins develop recipes/cookbooks on workstation</li> <li>Cookbooks uploaded to Chef Server</li> <li>Chef Clients pull configurations from server (typically every 30 mins)</li> <li>Clients execute recipes to configure systems</li> <li>Results reported back to server</li> </ol> <p><strong>Advantages</strong>: ✅ <strong>Flexible Ruby DSL</strong>: Full programming language for complex logic ✅ <strong>Test-driven infrastructure</strong>: ChefSpec and Test Kitchen for testing ✅ <strong>Scalable</strong>: Efficient at managing thousands of nodes ✅ <strong>Advanced orchestration</strong>: Chef Automate provides workflow capabilities</p> <p><strong>Disadvantages</strong>: ⚠️ <strong>Steep learning curve</strong>: Requires Ruby knowledge ⚠️ <strong>Complex setup</strong>: Server, workstation, client architecture ⚠️ <strong>Agent required</strong>: Must install and maintain Chef clients ⚠️ <strong>Overkill for simple tasks</strong>: Heavy for basic automation</p> <h2 id="configuration-language-and-syntax"> <a href="#configuration-language-and-syntax" title="Configuration Language and Syntax">Configuration Language and Syntax</a> <a href="#configuration-language-and-syntax" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Configuration Language and Syntax">#</a> </h2> <h3 id="ansible-yaml-playbooks"> <a href="#ansible-yaml-playbooks" title="Ansible: YAML Playbooks">Ansible: YAML Playbooks</a> <a href="#ansible-yaml-playbooks" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Ansible: YAML Playbooks">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>Syntax Style</strong>: Declarative-looking but procedural (order matters)</p> <p><strong>Example</strong> (install and start Apache):</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-yaml" data-lang="yaml"><span style="display:flex;"><span>--- </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>- <span style="color:#f92672">name</span>: <span style="color:#ae81ff">Configure web servers</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#f92672">hosts</span>: <span style="color:#ae81ff">webservers</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#f92672">become</span>: <span style="color:#66d9ef">yes</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#f92672">tasks</span>: </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> - <span style="color:#f92672">name</span>: <span style="color:#ae81ff">Install Apache</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#f92672">ansible.builtin.package</span>: </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#f92672">name</span>: <span style="color:#ae81ff">httpd</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#f92672">state</span>: <span style="color:#ae81ff">present</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> - <span style="color:#f92672">name</span>: <span style="color:#ae81ff">Start Apache service</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#f92672">ansible.builtin.service</span>: </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#f92672">name</span>: <span style="color:#ae81ff">httpd</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#f92672">state</span>: <span style="color:#ae81ff">started</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#f92672">enabled</span>: <span style="color:#66d9ef">yes</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> - <span style="color:#f92672">name</span>: <span style="color:#ae81ff">Deploy website</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#f92672">ansible.builtin.copy</span>: </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#f92672">src</span>: <span style="color:#ae81ff">./website/index.html</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#f92672">dest</span>: <span style="color:#ae81ff">/var/www/html/index.html</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#f92672">mode</span>: <span style="color:#e6db74">'0644'</span> </span></span></code></pre></div><p><strong>Pros</strong>: ✅ <strong>Readable</strong>: YAML is human-friendly and easy to learn ✅ <strong>No programming required</strong>: Simple syntax for common tasks ✅ <strong>Quick to write</strong>: Less verbose than alternatives</p> <p><strong>Cons</strong>: ⚠️ <strong>Limited logic</strong>: Complex conditionals can get messy ⚠️ <strong>Procedural</strong>: Order of tasks matters (can be error-prone) ⚠️ <strong>YAML limitations</strong>: Indentation-sensitive, no native data types</p> <h3 id="puppet-puppet-dsl-domain-specific-language"> <a href="#puppet-puppet-dsl-domain-specific-language" title="Puppet: Puppet DSL (Domain-Specific Language)">Puppet: Puppet DSL (Domain-Specific Language)</a> <a href="#puppet-puppet-dsl-domain-specific-language" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Puppet: Puppet DSL (Domain-Specific Language)">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>Syntax Style</strong>: Declarative (describe desired state)</p> <p><strong>Example</strong> (same Apache setup):</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-puppet" data-lang="puppet"><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#66d9ef">class</span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">apache</span> {<span style="color:#75715e"> </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"> # Install Apache package</span><span style="color:#960050;background-color:#1e0010"> </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">package</span> { <span style="color:#e6db74">'httpd'</span>:<span style="color:#960050;background-color:#1e0010"> </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">ensure</span> <span style="color:#f92672">=></span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">installed</span>,<span style="color:#960050;background-color:#1e0010"> </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> }<span style="color:#75715e"> </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"> </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"> # Manage Apache service</span><span style="color:#960050;background-color:#1e0010"> </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">service</span> { <span style="color:#e6db74">'httpd'</span>:<span style="color:#960050;background-color:#1e0010"> </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">ensure</span> <span style="color:#f92672">=></span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">running</span>,<span style="color:#960050;background-color:#1e0010"> </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">enable</span> <span style="color:#f92672">=></span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span>,<span style="color:#960050;background-color:#1e0010"> </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">require</span> <span style="color:#f92672">=></span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">Package</span>[<span style="color:#e6db74">'httpd'</span>],<span style="color:#960050;background-color:#1e0010"> </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> }<span style="color:#75715e"> </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"> </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"> # Deploy website file</span><span style="color:#960050;background-color:#1e0010"> </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">file</span> { <span style="color:#e6db74">'/var/www/html/index.html'</span>:<span style="color:#960050;background-color:#1e0010"> </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">ensure</span> <span style="color:#f92672">=></span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">file</span>,<span style="color:#960050;background-color:#1e0010"> </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">source</span> <span style="color:#f92672">=></span> <span style="color:#e6db74">'puppet:///modules/apache/index.html'</span>,<span style="color:#960050;background-color:#1e0010"> </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">mode</span> <span style="color:#f92672">=></span> <span style="color:#e6db74">'0644'</span>,<span style="color:#960050;background-color:#1e0010"> </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">require</span> <span style="color:#f92672">=></span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">Package</span>[<span style="color:#e6db74">'httpd'</span>],<span style="color:#960050;background-color:#1e0010"> </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> }<span style="color:#960050;background-color:#1e0010"> </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>}<span style="color:#960050;background-color:#1e0010"> </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#960050;background-color:#1e0010"> </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#66d9ef">node</span> <span style="color:#e6db74">'webserver.example.com'</span> {<span style="color:#960050;background-color:#1e0010"> </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">include</span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">apache</span><span style="color:#960050;background-color:#1e0010"> </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>}<span style="color:#960050;background-color:#1e0010"> </span></span></span></code></pre></div><p><strong>Pros</strong>: ✅ <strong>True declarative</strong>: Describe
