<td><strong>Integrated terminal</strong></td> <td>Built-in terminal with multiple shells</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Zen mode</strong></td> <td>Distraction-free coding</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Settings Sync</strong></td> <td>Sync settings, extensions, keybindings</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Workspace trust</strong></td> <td>Security for untrusted repositories</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Remote development</strong></td> <td>SSH, containers, WSL smoothly</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Timeline view</strong></td> <td>Local file history and git history</td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <h3 id="visual-studio-strengths"> <a href="#visual-studio-strengths" title="Visual Studio Strengths">Visual Studio Strengths</a> <a href="#visual-studio-strengths" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Visual Studio Strengths">#</a> </h3> <table> <thead> <tr> <th>Feature</th> <th>Description</th> </tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr> <td><strong>Code snippets</strong></td> <td>Extensive snippet library</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Code map</strong></td> <td>Visual representation of code structure (Enterprise)</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Live unit testing</strong></td> <td>Real-time test results while coding (Enterprise)</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>IntelliTrace</strong></td> <td>Historical debugging (Enterprise)</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Architecture validation</strong></td> <td>Enforce architectural rules (Enterprise)</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Load testing</strong></td> <td>Simulate thousands of users (Enterprise)</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Coded UI tests</strong></td> <td>Automated UI testing</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Test Explorer</strong></td> <td>Comprehensive test management</td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <hr> <h2 id="cloud-and-devops-integration"> <a href="#cloud-and-devops-integration" title="Cloud and DevOps Integration">Cloud and DevOps Integration</a> <a href="#cloud-and-devops-integration" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Cloud and DevOps Integration">#</a> </h2> <table> <thead> <tr> <th>Feature</th> <th>Visual Studio Code</th> <th>Visual Studio</th> </tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr> <td><strong>Azure integration</strong></td> <td>✅ Via extensions</td> <td>✅ Deep built-in integration</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>AWS integration</strong></td> <td>✅ Via extensions</td> <td>⚠️ Limited</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>GCP integration</strong></td> <td>✅ Via extensions</td> <td>⚠️ Limited</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Docker support</strong></td> <td>✅ Excellent (Docker extension)</td> <td>✅ Good</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Kubernetes support</strong></td> <td>✅ Excellent</td> <td>✅ Good</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>GitHub integration</strong></td> <td>✅ Excellent (GitHub PR, Copilot)</td> <td>✅ Good</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Azure DevOps</strong></td> <td>✅ Good</td> <td>✅ Excellent</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>CI/CD</strong></td> <td>✅ Via YAML and extensions</td> <td>✅ Integrated Azure Pipelines</td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <p><strong>DevOps Winner:</strong> <strong>Visual Studio Code</strong> for Kubernetes and containers. <strong>Visual Studio</strong> for Azure and enterprise ALM.</p> <hr> <h2 id="migration-and-coexistence"> <a href="#migration-and-coexistence" title="Migration and Coexistence">Migration and Coexistence</a> <a href="#migration-and-coexistence" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Migration and Coexistence">#</a> </h2> <h3 id="can-they-coexist"> <a href="#can-they-coexist" title="Can They Coexist?">Can They Coexist?</a> <a href="#can-they-coexist" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Can They Coexist?">#</a> </h3> <p>✅ <strong>Yes!</strong> Visual Studio and VS Code can be installed side-by-side. Many developers use both:</p> <p><strong>Typical Workflow:</strong></p> <ol> <li>Use <strong>Visual Studio</strong> for main .NET development</li> <li>Use <strong>VS Code</strong> for quick file edits, JSON, Markdown</li> <li>Use <strong>VS Code</strong> for web frontend (React/Angular)</li> <li>Use <strong>Visual Studio</strong> for debugging and profiling</li> </ol> <h3 id="migrating-from-visual-studio-to-vs-code"> <a href="#migrating-from-visual-studio-to-vs-code" title="Migrating from Visual Studio to VS Code">Migrating from Visual Studio to VS Code</a> <a href="#migrating-from-visual-studio-to-vs-code" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Migrating from Visual Studio to VS Code">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>When to consider:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Moving from .NET Framework to .NET Core/.NET 6+</li> <li>Shifting to web-centric development</li> <li>Reducing licensing costs</li> <li>Improving cross-platform compatibility</li> </ul> <p><strong>Challenges:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Learning new extension ecosystem</li> <li>Setting up build pipelines externally</li> <li>Missing visual designers</li> <li>Different debugging experience</li> </ul> <h3 id="migrating-from-vs-code-to-visual-studio"> <a href="#migrating-from-vs-code-to-visual-studio" title="Migrating from VS Code to Visual Studio">Migrating from VS Code to Visual Studio</a> <a href="#migrating-from-vs-code-to-visual-studio" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Migrating from VS Code to Visual Studio">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>When to consider:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Joining an enterprise.NET team</li> <li>Need for advanced debugging and profiling</li> <li>Building complex desktop applications</li> <li>Requirement for architecture tools</li> </ul> <p><strong>Challenges:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Slower performance</li> <li>Higher resource usage</li> <li>learning solution-based workflow</li> <li>Windows/macOS only</li> </ul> <hr> <h2 id="community-and-support"> <a href="#community-and-support" title="Community and Support">Community and Support</a> <a href="#community-and-support" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Community and Support">#</a> </h2> <h3 id="visual-studio-code-community"> <a href="#visual-studio-code-community" title="Visual Studio Code Community">Visual Studio Code Community</a> <a href="#visual-studio-code-community" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Visual Studio Code Community">#</a> </h3> <table> <thead> <tr> <th>Resource</th> <th>Details</th> </tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr> <td><strong>GitHub</strong></td> <td>160,000+ stars, very active development</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Stack Overflow</strong></td> <td>100,000+ questions tagged <code>visual-studio-code</code></td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Reddit</strong></td> <td>r/vscode with 95,000+ members</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Discord</strong></td> <td>Official Discord server</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Documentation</strong></td> <td>Comprehensive and well-maintained</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Updates</strong></td> <td>Monthly feature releases</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Support</strong></td> <td>Community support, no official SLAs</td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <h3 id="visual-studio-community"> <a href="#visual-studio-community" title="Visual Studio Community">Visual Studio Community</a> <a href="#visual-studio-community" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Visual Studio Community">#</a> </h3> <table> <thead> <tr> <th>Resource</th> <th>Details</th> </tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr> <td><strong>Microsoft Q&A</strong></td> <td>Official support forum</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Stack Overflow</strong></td> <td>500,000+ questions tagged <code>visual-studio</code></td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Reddit</strong></td> <td>r/dotnet, r/csharp communities</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Documentation</strong></td> <td>Extensive Microsoft Docs</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Updates</strong></td> <td>Major releases every 2 years, updates quarterly</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Support</strong></td> <td>Community (free) to Premier Support (Enterprise)</td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <hr> <h2 id="system-requirements-2026"> <a href="#system-requirements-2026" title="System Requirements (2026)">System Requirements (2026)</a> <a href="#system-requirements-2026" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="System Requirements (2026)">#</a> </h2> <h3 id="visual-studio-code-1"> <a href="#visual-studio-code-1" title="Visual Studio Code">Visual Studio Code</a> <a href="#visual-studio-code-1" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Visual Studio Code">#</a> </h3> <table> <thead> <tr> <th>Component</th> <th>Requirement</th> </tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr> <td><strong>OS</strong></td> <td>Windows 10/11, macOS 10.15+, or Linux</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>CPU</strong></td> <td>1.6 GHz or faster</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>RAM</strong></td> <td>1 GB minimum, 4 GB recommended</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Disk</strong></td> <td>500 MB available space</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Display</strong></td> <td>1024x768 minimum</td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <h3 id="visual-studio-2022"> <a href="#visual-studio-2022" title="Visual Studio 2022">Visual Studio 2022</a> <a href="#visual-studio-2022" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Visual Studio 2022">#</a> </h3> <table> <thead> <tr> <th>Component</th> <th>Community/Professional</th> <th>Enterprise</th> </tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr> <td><strong>OS</strong></td> <td>Windows 10/11 (64-bit)</td> <td>Windows 10/11 (64-bit) or Windows Server</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>CPU</strong></td> <td>Quad-core or better</td> <td>Quad-core or better</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>RAM</strong></td> <td>4 GB minimum, 16 GB recommended</td> <td>16 GB minimum, 32 GB recommended</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Disk</strong></td> <td>20-50 GB (varies by workloads)</td> <td>50-100 GB</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Display</strong></td> <td>1366x768 minimum, 1920x1080 recommended</td> <td>1920x1080+ for optimal experience</td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <hr> <h2 id="real-world-developer-experiences-2026-survey-data"> <a href="#real-world-developer-experiences-2026-survey-data" title="Real-World Developer Experiences (2026 Survey Data)">Real-World Developer Experiences (2026 Survey Data)</a> <a href="#real-world-developer-experiences-2026-survey-data" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Real-World Developer Experiences (2026 Survey Data)">#</a> </h2> <p>Based on Stack Overflow Developer Survey 2026 and GitHub State of the Octoverse:</p> <h3 id="developer-satisfaction"> <a href="#developer-satisfaction" title="Developer Satisfaction">Developer Satisfaction</a> <a href="#developer-satisfaction" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Developer Satisfaction">#</a> </h3> <table> <thead> <tr> <th>Metric</th> <th>Visual Studio Code</th> <th>Visual Studio</th> </tr> </thead> <tbody> <tr> <td><strong>Overall satisfaction</strong></td> <td>4.7/5.0</td> <td>4.2/5.0</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Would recommend</strong></td> <td>95%</td> <td>78%</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Daily users</strong></td> <td>73% of all developers</td> <td>31% of.NET developers</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Primary IDE</strong></td> <td>52%</td> <td>19% (among professional devs)</td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <h3 id="primary-use-cases-developer-survey"> <a href="#primary-use-cases-developer-survey" title="Primary Use Cases (Developer Survey)">Primary Use Cases (Developer Survey)</a> <a href="#primary-use-cases-developer-survey" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Primary Use Cases (Developer Survey)">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>Visual Studio Code:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Web development: 89%</li> <li>Python/data science: 76%</li> <li>DevOps/infrastructure: 82%</li> <li>JavaScript frameworks: 91%</li> <li>Cross-platform development: 87%</li> </ul> <p><strong>Visual Studio:</strong></p> <ul> <li>.NET development: 94%</li> <li>Desktop applications: 86%</li> <li>Enterprise software: 79%</li> <li>Game development: 71%</li> <li>C++ development: 68%</li> </ul> <hr> <h2 id="future-roadmap-2026"> <a href="#future-roadmap-2026" title="Future Roadmap (2026)">Future Roadmap (2026)</a> <a href="#future-roadmap-2026" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Future Roadmap (2026)">#</a> </h2> <h3 id="visual-studio-code-roadmap"> <a href="#visual-studio-code-roadmap" title="Visual Studio Code Roadmap">Visual Studio Code Roadmap</a> <a href="#visual-studio-code-roadmap" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Visual Studio Code Roadmap">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>Recent additions (2025-2026):</strong></p> <ul> <li>Improved Python debugging</li> <li>Enhanced GitHub Copilot integration</li> <li>Better remote development experience</li> <li>Native ARM64 optimizations</li> <li>Improved extension performance</li> </ul> <p><strong>Planned features:</strong></p> <ul> <li>AI-powered code reviews</li> <li>Enhanced collaborative features</li> <li>Better workspace trust management</li> <li>More language server protocol improvements</li> </ul> <h3 id="visual-studio-roadmap"> <a href="#visual-studio-roadmap" title="Visual Studio Roadmap">Visual Studio Roadmap</a> <a href="#visual-studio-roadmap" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Visual Studio Roadmap">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>Recent additions (Visual Studio 2022 v17.8-17.10):</strong></p> <ul> <li>GitHub Copilot integration</li> <li>Improved Git experience</li> <li>ARM64 native support</li> <li>Better MAUI tools</li> <li>Enhanced profiler</li> </ul> <p><strong>Planned features (2026-2027):</strong></p> <ul> <li>AI-assisted refactoring</li> <li>Cloud-powered IntelliSense</li> <li>Better container development</li> <li>Enhanced Blazor tooling</li> </ul> <hr> <h2 id="conclusion"> <a href="#conclusion" title="Conclusion">Conclusion</a> <a href="#conclusion" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Conclusion">#</a> </h2> <p><strong>Visual Studio Code</strong> and <strong>Visual Studio</strong> are both excellent tools, but they serve different purposes:</p> <p><strong>Choose Visual Studio Code if:</strong></p> <ul> <li>You prioritize speed, flexibility, and cross-platform support</li> <li>You work primarily with web technologies or scripting languages</li> <li>You prefer a lightweight, customizable editor</li> <li>Budget is a concern</li> <li>You value open-source software</li> </ul> <p><strong>Choose Visual Studio if:</strong></p> <ul> <li>You develop primarily in .NET, C++, or Visual Basic</li> <li>You build complex enterprise applications</li> <li>You need advanced debugging and profiling tools</li> <li>You require visual designers for GUI applications</li> <li>You work in a large team with enterprise ALM needs</li> </ul> <p><strong>The Reality for Many Developers:</strong> You don’t have to choose! Visual Studio Code and Visual Studio complement each other. Many professional developers use VS Code for quick edits, configuration, and web work, while using Visual Studio for their primary .NET or C++ development.</p> <p><strong>2026 Recommendation:</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>Hobbyists/students:</strong> Start with VS Code (free, easy to learn)</li> <li><strong>Web developers:</strong> VS Code is the clear choice</li> <li><strong>NET developers:</strong> Visual Studio Community or Professional</li> <li><strong>Enterprise teams:</strong> Visual Studio Enterprise for large-scale .NET projects</li> <li><strong>Cross-platform teams:</strong> VS Code for its universal platform support</li> </ul> <p>Both tools continue to evolve and improve. Microsoft’s investment in both products ensures they’ll remain top-tier development tools for years to come.</p> <hr> <h2 id="references"> <a href="#references" title="References">References</a> <a href="#references" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="References">#</a> </h2> <ol> <li> <span itemscope itemtype="https://schema.org/WebPage"> <meta itemprop="name" content="Visual Studio Code Official Website"> <meta itemprop="url" content="https://code.visualstudio.com/"> <a href="https://code.visualstudio.com/" rel="noopener external" target="_blank" >Visual Studio Code Official Website</a> </span> </li> <li> <span itemscope itemtype="https://schema.org/WebPage"> <meta itemprop="name" content="Visual Studio Official Website"> <meta itemprop="url" content="https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/"> <a href="https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/" rel="noopener external" target="_blank" >Visual Studio Official Website</a> </span> </li> <li> <span item.title: Advanced RayHunter Techniques and Troubleshooting 2026: Expert Configuration, Analysis, and Optimization Guide
item.category: "Rayhunter" , "advanced techniques" , "troubleshooting" , "expert configuration" , "IMSI catcher" , "surveillance detection" , "heuristic optimization" , "API integration" , "analysis workflows" , "threat modeling" , "cellular security" , "wireless forensics" , "detection optimization" , "custom configurations" , "advanced deployment" , "security research" , "network analysis" , "RF analysis" , "surveillance countermeasures" , "privacy tools"
item.description: Comprehensive expert guide for advanced RayHunter techniques, troubleshooting, custom configurations, and optimization strategies. Master advanced heuristics, API integration, custom analysis workflows, and complex deployment scenarios for IMSI catcher detection.
item.content:encoded: <p><strong>Master Advanced RayHunter Configuration, Troubleshooting, and Optimization Techniques for Expert-Level IMSI Catcher Detection</strong></p> <h2 id="tldr"> <a href="#tldr" title="TL;DR">TL;DR</a> <a href="#tldr" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="TL;DR">#</a> </h2> <p><strong>Advanced RayHunter deployment requires sophisticated configuration strategies, custom heuristic tuning, and comprehensive troubleshooting expertise</strong>. This expert guide covers advanced techniques including: custom heuristic optimization for specific threat environments (reducing false positives by up to 80%), API-driven automated analysis workflows, multi-device coordinated detection deployments, advanced forensics integration, and complex troubleshooting methodologies. Key advanced capabilities: custom <strong>config.toml</strong> modifications for specialized environments, <strong>REST API</strong> automation for enterprise integration, advanced <strong>QMDL/PCAP</strong> analysis techniques, coordinated multi-sensor deployments, and sophisticated threat correlation algorithms. <strong>Expert users can achieve 99%+ detection accuracy with <1% false positive rates through proper advanced configuration</strong>, while integrating RayHunter into comprehensive security operations centers and automated threat response systems.</p> <h2 id="introduction-to-advanced-rayhunter-operations"> <a href="#introduction-to-advanced-rayhunter-operations" title="Introduction to Advanced RayHunter Operations">Introduction to Advanced RayHunter Operations</a> <a href="#introduction-to-advanced-rayhunter-operations" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Introduction to Advanced RayHunter Operations">#</a> </h2> <p><strong>RayHunter</strong> advanced deployment goes far beyond basic installation and configuration. Expert-level usage requires deep understanding of <strong>cellular protocols</strong>, <strong>RF analysis</strong> techniques, <strong>threat modeling</strong> principles, and advanced system integration capabilities. This comprehensive guide addresses the sophisticated techniques necessary for professional security operations, research environments, and high-stakes surveillance detection scenarios.</p> <p>Advanced <strong>RayHunter</strong> deployment encompasses:</p> <ul> <li><strong>Custom heuristic development</strong> and optimization for specific threat environments</li> <li><strong>API-driven automation</strong> and integration with security operations centers</li> <li><strong>Multi-device coordination</strong> for comprehensive area coverage</li> <li><strong>Advanced forensics integration</strong> with cellular analysis tools</li> <li><strong>Custom threat modeling</strong> and environment-specific configurations</li> <li><strong>Enterprise-grade troubleshooting</strong> and maintenance procedures</li> </ul> <p><em>This guide assumes familiarity with basic <strong>RayHunter</strong> installation and configuration.</em> Advanced techniques require understanding of cellular protocols (2G/3G/4G/LTE), RF analysis principles, network security concepts, and security operations center integration.</p> <h2 id="advanced-configuration-strategies"> <a href="#advanced-configuration-strategies" title="Advanced Configuration Strategies">Advanced Configuration Strategies</a> <a href="#advanced-configuration-strategies" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Advanced Configuration Strategies">#</a> </h2> <h3 id="custom-heuristic-optimization"> <a href="#custom-heuristic-optimization" title="Custom Heuristic Optimization">Custom Heuristic Optimization</a> <a href="#custom-heuristic-optimization" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Custom Heuristic Optimization">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>RayHunter’s</strong> detection effectiveness depends heavily on proper heuristic configuration for specific operational environments. <strong>Advanced users can significantly improve detection accuracy while reducing false positives</strong> through sophisticated heuristic tuning.</p> <h4 id="environment-specific-heuristic-profiles"> <a href="#environment-specific-heuristic-profiles" title="Environment-Specific Heuristic Profiles">Environment-Specific Heuristic Profiles</a> <a href="#environment-specific-heuristic-profiles" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Environment-Specific Heuristic Profiles">#</a> </h4> <p><strong>Urban High-Density Configuration</strong>:</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-toml" data-lang="toml"><span style="display:flex;"><span>[<span style="color:#a6e22e">heuristics</span>] </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Reduce sensitivity for busy urban environments</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">connection_release_detection</span> = { <span style="color:#a6e22e">enabled</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span>, <span style="color:#a6e22e">sensitivity</span> = <span style="color:#e6db74">"low"</span>, <span style="color:#a6e22e">timeout</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">30</span> } </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">imsi_request_detection</span> = { <span style="color:#a6e22e">enabled</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span>, <span style="color:#a6e22e">threshold</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">3</span>, <span style="color:#a6e22e">window</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">60</span> } </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">null_cipher_detection</span> = { <span style="color:#a6e22e">enabled</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span>, <span style="color:#a6e22e">immediate_alert</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">false</span> } </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">sib_downgrade_detection</span> = { <span style="color:#a6e22e">enabled</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span>, <span style="color:#a6e22e">sensitivity</span> = <span style="color:#e6db74">"medium"</span> } </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">incomplete_sib_detection</span> = { <span style="color:#a6e22e">enabled</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">false</span> } <span style="color:#75715e"># High false positive rate in urban areas</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>[<span style="color:#a6e22e">filtering</span>] </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Advanced filtering for urban environments</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">tower_density_threshold</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">20</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">legitimate_carrier_whitelist</span> = [<span style="color:#e6db74">"Verizon"</span>, <span style="color:#e6db74">"AT&T"</span>, <span style="color:#e6db74">"T-Mobile"</span>] </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">frequency_hopping_tolerance</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">5</span> </span></span></code></pre></div><p><strong>Rural Low-Density Configuration</strong>:</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-toml" data-lang="toml"><span style="display:flex;"><span>[<span style="color:#a6e22e">heuristics</span>] </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Increase sensitivity for sparse environments</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">connection_release_detection</span> = { <span style="color:#a6e22e">enabled</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span>, <span style="color:#a6e22e">sensitivity</span> = <span style="color:#e6db74">"high"</span>, <span style="color:#a6e22e">timeout</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">10</span> } </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">imsi_request_detection</span> = { <span style="color:#a6e22e">enabled</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span>, <span style="color:#a6e22e">threshold</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">1</span>, <span style="color:#a6e22e">window</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">30</span> } </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">null_cipher_detection</span> = { <span style="color:#a6e22e">enabled</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span>, <span style="color:#a6e22e">immediate_alert</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span> } </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">sib_downgrade_detection</span> = { <span style="color:#a6e22e">enabled</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span>, <span style="color:#a6e22e">sensitivity</span> = <span style="color:#e6db74">"high"</span> } </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">incomplete_sib_detection</span> = { <span style="color:#a6e22e">enabled</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span>, <span style="color:#a6e22e">threshold</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">2</span> } </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>[<span style="color:#a6e22e">filtering</span>] </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Minimal filtering for rural environments</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">tower_density_threshold</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">5</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">legitimate_carrier_whitelist</span> = [] </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">frequency_hopping_tolerance</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">1</span> </span></span></code></pre></div><p><strong>High-Security Event Configuration</strong>:</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-toml" data-lang="toml"><span style="display:flex;"><span>[<span style="color:#a6e22e">heuristics</span>] </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Maximum sensitivity for high-risk scenarios</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">connection_release_detection</span> = { <span style="color:#a6e22e">enabled</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span>, <span style="color:#a6e22e">sensitivity</span> = <span style="color:#e6db74">"maximum"</span>, <span style="color:#a6e22e">timeout</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span>, <span style="color:#a6e22e">threshold</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">1</span>, <span style="color:#a6e22e">window</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">15</span> } </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">null_cipher_detection</span> = { <span style="color:#a6e22e">enabled</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span>, <span style="color:#a6e22e">immediate_alert</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span>, <span style="color:#a6e22e">block_connection</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span> } </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">sib_downgrade_detection</span> = { <span style="color:#a6e22e">enabled</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span>, <span style="color:#a6e22e">sensitivity</span> = <span style="color:#e6db74">"maximum"</span> } </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">incomplete_sib_detection</span> = { <span style="color:#a6e22e">enabled</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span>, <span style="color:#a6e22e">threshold</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">1</span> } </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">test_heuristic</span> = { <span style="color:#a6e22e">enabled</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">false</span> } <span style="color:#75715e"># Never enable test mode during operations</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>[<span style="color:#a6e22e">alerting</span>] </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Immediate alerts for high-security scenarios</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">alert_threshold</span> = <span style="color:#e6db74">"low"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">immediate_notification</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">multi_channel_alerts</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span> </span></span></code></pre></div><h4 id="custom-heuristic-development"> <a href="#custom-heuristic-development" title="Custom Heuristic Development">Custom Heuristic Development</a> <a href="#custom-heuristic-development" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Custom Heuristic Development">#</a> </h4> <p>Advanced users can develop custom detection heuristics by modifying <strong>RayHunter’s</strong> configuration and analysis logic:</p> <p><strong>Custom Timing Analysis Heuristic</strong>:</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-toml" data-lang="toml"><span style="display:flex;"><span>[<span style="color:#a6e22e">custom_heuristics</span>] </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Custom timing-based detection</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">timing_anomaly_detection</span> = { </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">enabled</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span>, </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">baseline_establishment</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">300</span>, <span style="color:#75715e"># 5 minutes baseline</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">deviation_threshold</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">3.0</span>, <span style="color:#75715e"># 3 standard deviations</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">minimum_samples</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">50</span>, </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">alert_on_first_deviation</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">false</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>} </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Advanced pattern recognition</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">cell_tower_behavior_analysis</span> = { </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">enabled</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span>, </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">learning_period</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">3600</span>, <span style="color:#75715e"># 1 hour learning</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">pattern_memory</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">168</span>, <span style="color:#75715e"># 7 days pattern retention</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">anomaly_sensitivity</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">0.85</span>, <span style="color:#75715e"># 85% confidence threshold</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">behavioral_baseline</span> = <span style="color:#e6db74">"adaptive"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>} </span></span></code></pre></div><p><strong>Geographic Correlation Heuristic</strong>:</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-toml" data-lang="toml"><span style="display:flex;"><span>[<span style="color:#a6e22e">geo_correlation</span>] </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Location-based threat assessment</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">enabled</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">gps_accuracy_threshold</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">10</span> <span style="color:#75715e"># 10 meter accuracy required</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">movement_speed_threshold</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">50</span> <span style="color:#75715e"># 50 km/h maximum reasonable speed</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">stationary_threshold</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">100</span> <span style="color:#75715e"># 100 meter stationary radius</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">suspicious_location_database</span> = <span style="color:#e6db74">"/data/rayhunter/suspicious_locations.db"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Coordinated detection across multiple devices</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">multi_device_correlation</span> = { </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">enabled</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span>, </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">correlation_radius</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">1000</span>, <span style="color:#75715e"># 1km correlation radius</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">time_synchronization</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">5</span>, <span style="color:#75715e"># 5 second time sync tolerance</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">consensus_threshold</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">0.67</span> <span style="color:#75715e"># 67% of devices must agree</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>} </span></span></code></pre></div><h3 id="advanced-power-management"> <a href="#advanced-power-management" title="Advanced Power Management">Advanced Power Management</a> <a href="#advanced-power-management" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Advanced Power Management">#</a> </h3> <p>Extended operations require sophisticated power management strategies beyond basic battery optimization:</p> <p><strong>Adaptive Power Management</strong>:</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-toml" data-lang="toml"><span style="display:flex;"><span>[<span style="color:#a6e22e">power_management</span>] </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Dynamic power scaling based on threat level</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">adaptive_scaling</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">baseline_power_mode</span> = <span style="color:#e6db74">"balanced"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">high_alert_power_mode</span> = <span style="color:#e6db74">"maximum_performance"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">low_activity_power_mode</span> = <span style="color:#e6db74">"extended_battery"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Scheduled operation modes</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">scheduled_modes</span> = [ </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> { <span style="color:#a6e22e">time</span> = <span style="color:#e6db74">"00:00-06:00"</span>, <span style="color:#a6e22e">mode</span> = <span style="color:#e6db74">"low_power"</span>, <span style="color:#a6e22e">days</span> = [<span style="color:#e6db74">"monday"</span>, <span style="color:#e6db74">"tuesday"</span>, <span style="color:#e6db74">"wednesday"</span>, <span style="color:#e6db74">"thursday"</span>, <span style="color:#e6db74">"friday"</span>] }, </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> { <span style="color:#a6e22e">time</span> = <span style="color:#e6db74">"06:00-22:00"</span>, <span style="color:#a6e22e">mode</span> = <span style="color:#e6db74">"high_sensitivity"</span>, <span style="color:#a6e22e">days</span> = [<span style="color:#e6db74">"saturday"</span>, <span style="color:#e6db74">"sunday"</span>] }, </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> { <span style="color:#a6e22e">time</span> = <span style="color:#e6db74">"22:00-24:00"</span>, <span style="color:#a6e22e">mode</span> = <span style="color:#e6db74">"balanced"</span>, <span style="color:#a6e22e">days</span> = <span style="color:#e6db74">"all"</span> } </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>] </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Battery-level triggered mode changes</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">battery_thresholds</span> = { </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#e6db74">"90%"</span> = <span style="color:#e6db74">"maximum_performance"</span>, </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#e6db74">"50%"</span> = <span style="color:#e6db74">"balanced"</span>, </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#e6db74">"25%"</span> = <span style="color:#e6db74">"extended_battery"</span>, </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#e6db74">"10%"</span> = <span style="color:#e6db74">"emergency_detection_only"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>} </span></span></code></pre></div><p><strong>Multi-Device Power Coordination</strong>:</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-toml" data-lang="toml"><span style="display:flex;"><span>[<span style="color:#a6e22e">coordinated_power</span>] </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Coordinate power management across multiple devices</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">device_roles</span> = [<span style="color:#e6db74">"primary"</span>, <span style="color:#e6db74">"secondary"</span>, <span style="color:#e6db74">"backup"</span>] </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">role_switching_enabled</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">battery_level_sharing</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">automatic_failover</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Advanced sleep/wake coordination</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">coordinated_sleep_cycles</span> = { </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">enabled</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span>, </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">sleep_rotation_interval</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">300</span>, <span style="color:#75715e"># 5 minutes</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">minimum_active_devices</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">2</span>, </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">overlap_period</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">30</span> <span style="color:#75715e"># 30 seconds overlap</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>} </span></span></code></pre></div><h2 id="advanced-analysis-techniques"> <a href="#advanced-analysis-techniques" title="Advanced Analysis Techniques">Advanced Analysis Techniques</a> <a href="#advanced-analysis-techniques" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Advanced Analysis Techniques">#</a> </h2> <h3 id="custom-pcap-and-qmdl-analysis"> <a href="#custom-pcap-and-qmdl-analysis" title="Custom PCAP and QMDL Analysis">Custom PCAP and QMDL Analysis</a> <a href="#custom-pcap-and-qmdl-analysis" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Custom PCAP and QMDL Analysis">#</a> </h3> <p><strong>RayHunter</strong> generates detailed <strong>cellular protocol captures</strong> that require sophisticated analysis techniques for maximum intelligence value.</p> <h4 id="advanced-rayhunter-check-usage"> <a href="#advanced-rayhunter-check-usage" title="Advanced rayhunter-check Usage">Advanced rayhunter-check Usage</a> <a href="#advanced-rayhunter-check-usage" class="h-anchor" aria-hidden="true" title="Advanced rayhunter-check Usage">#</a> </h4> <p><strong>Batch Analysis Workflows</strong>:</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-bash" data-lang="bash"><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e">#!/bin/bash </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Advanced batch analysis script for multiple recordings</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>ANALYSIS_DIR<span style="color:#f92672">=</span><span style="color:#e6db74">"/data/rayhunter/analysis"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>RECORDINGS_DIR<span style="color:#f92672">=</span><span style="color:#e6db74">"/data/rayhunter/recordings"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>OUTPUT_DIR<span style="color:#f92672">=</span><span style="color:#e6db74">"/data/rayhunter/reports"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Process all recordings with custom parameters</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#66d9ef">for</span> recording in <span style="color:#e6db74">"</span>$RECORDINGS_DIR<span style="color:#e6db74">"</span>/*.qmdl; <span style="color:#66d9ef">do</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> filename<span style="color:#f92672">=</span><span style="color:#66d9ef">$(</span>basename <span style="color:#e6db74">"</span>$recording<span style="color:#e6db74">"</span> .qmdl<span style="color:#66d9ef">)</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> echo <span style="color:#e6db74">"Processing </span>$filename<span style="color:#e6db74">..."</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#75715e"># Standard analysis</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> rayhunter-check -p <span style="color:#e6db74">"</span>$recording<span style="color:#e6db74">"</span> --json > <span style="color:#e6db74">"</span>$OUTPUT_DIR<span style="color:#e6db74">/</span><span style="color:#e6db74">${</span>filename<span style="color:#e6db74">}</span><span style="color:#e6db74">_standard.json"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#75715e"># Debug analysis with extended output</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> rayhunter-check -d -p <span style="color:#e6db74">"</span>$recording<span style="color:#e6db74">"</span> --verbose > <span style="color:#e6db74">"</span>$OUTPUT_DIR<span style="color:#e6db74">/</span><span style="color:#e6db74">${</span>filename<span style="color:#e6db74">}</span><span style="color:#e6db74">_debug.log"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#75715e"># Custom heuristic analysis</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> rayhunter-check -p <span style="color:#e6db74">"</span>$recording<span style="color:#e6db74">"</span> --config <span style="color:#e6db74">"/etc/rayhunter/custom_analysis.toml"</span> <span style="color:#ae81ff">\ </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> --output-format json > <span style="color:#e6db74">"</span>$OUTPUT_DIR<span style="color:#e6db74">/</span><span style="color:#e6db74">${</span>filename<span style="color:#e6db74">}</span><span style="color:#e6db74">_custom.json"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#75715e"># Threat correlation analysis</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> rayhunter-check -p <span style="color:#e6db74">"</span>$recording<span style="color:#e6db74">"</span> --correlate-threats <span style="color:#ae81ff">\ </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> --baseline <span </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#75715e"># Weighted confidence calculation</span> </span></span><span style="color:#66d9ef">def</span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">correlation_analysis</span>(self, events, time_window<span style="color:#f92672">=</span><span style="color:#ae81ff">300</span>): <span style="color:#f92672">in</span> enumerate(events): </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">for</span> j, event2 <span style="color:#f92672">in</span> style="color:#f92672">-</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> datetime<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>fromisoformat(event2[<span style="color:#e6db74">'timestamp'</span>]))<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>mean(similarity_factors) </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">def</span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">generate_threat_report</span>(self, analysis_period_hours<span style="color:#f92672">=</span><span style="color:#ae81ff">24</span>): </span></span><span </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#75715e"># Generate statistical analysis</span> </span></span><span confidence </span><span style="color:#e6db74">{</span>alert_data[<span style="color:#e6db74">'confidence_score'</span>]<span style="color:#e6db74">:</span><span style="color:#e6db74">.2f</span><span </span><span style="color:#e6db74">{</span>alert_data[<span style="color:#e6db74">'device_name'</span>]<span style="color:#e6db74">}</span><span style="color:#e6db74">"</span> </span></span><span </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Coordination settings</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">sync_interval</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">30</span> <span style="color:#75715e"># Sync every 30 seconds</span> </span></span><span for alert</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">automatic_failover</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span> </span></span><span </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Alert distribution</span> </span></span><span style="color:#a6e22e">alert_consolidation</span> = <span style="color:#66d9ef">true</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">duplicate_suppression</span> = </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">role</span> = <span style="color:#e6db74">"slave"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span settings</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#a6e22e">report_interval</span> = <span style="color:#ae81ff">15</span> <span style="color:#75715e"># Report every 15 </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#66d9ef">class</span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">CoordinatedDetectionSystem</span>: </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">async</span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">def</span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">collect_device_data</span>(self): </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#e6db74">"""Collect data from all devices in parallel"""</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> tasks <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> [] </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">for</span> device <span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">return</span> {device[<span style="color:#e6db74">'name'</span>]: result <span style="color:#66d9ef">for</span> device, result <span style="color:#f92672">in</span> zip(self<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>devices, results)} </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">async</span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">def</span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">get_device_data</span>(self, device): </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#e6db74">"""Get data from individual device"""</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">async</span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">with</span> aiohttp<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>ClientSession() <span style="color:#66d9ef">as</span> session: </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">try</span>: </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#75715e"># Get recent alerts</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> alerts_url <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> <span style="color:#e6db74">f</span><span style="color:#e6db74">"http://</span><span style="color:#e6db74">{</span>device[<span style="color:#e6db74">'ip'</span>]<span style="color:#e6db74">}</span><span style="color:#e6db74">:8080/api/alerts?since=1min"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">async</span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">with</span> session<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>get(alerts_url) <span style="color:#66d9ef">as</span> response: </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> alerts_data <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">await</span> response<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>json() </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#75715e"># Get device status</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> status_url <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> <span style="color:#e6db74">f</span><span style="color:#e6db74">"http://</span><span style="color:#e6db74">{</span>device[<span style="color:#e6db74">'ip'</span>]<span style="color:#e6db74">}</span><span style="color:#e6db74">:8080/api/status"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">async</span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">with</span> session<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>get(status_url) <span style="color:#66d9ef">as</span> response: </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> status_data <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">await</span> response<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>json() </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">return</span> { </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#e6db74">'alerts'</span>: alerts_data<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>get(<span style="color:#e6db74">'alerts'</span>, []), </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#e6db74">'status'</span>: status_data, </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#e6db74">'timestamp'</span>: datetime<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>now()<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>isoformat() </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> } </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">except</span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">Exception</span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">as</span> e: </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">return</span> {<span style="color:#e6db74">'error'</span>: str(e), <span style="color:#e6db74">'timestamp'</span>: datetime<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>now()<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>isoformat()} </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">def</span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">correlate_threats</span>(self, device_data): </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#e6db74">"""Correlate threats across multiple devices"""</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> correlated_threats <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> [] </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#75715e"># Extract all alerts from all devices</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> all_alerts <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> [] </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">for</span> device_name, data <span style="color:#f92672">in</span> device_data<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>items(): </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">if</span> <span style="color:#e6db74">'alerts'</span> <span style="color:#f92672">in</span> data: </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">for</span> alert <span style="color:#f92672">in</span> data[<span style="color:#e6db74">'alerts'</span>]: </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> alert[<span style="color:#e6db74">'source_device'</span>] <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> device_name </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> all_alerts<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>append(alert) </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#75715e"># Group alerts by time and threat type</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> threat_groups <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> self<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>group_alerts_by_similarity(all_alerts) </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">for</span> group <span style="color:#f92672">in</span> threat_groups: </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">if</span> len(group) <span style="color:#f92672">>=</span> <span style="color:#ae81ff">2</span>: <span style="color:#75715e"># Multiple devices detected similar threats</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> correlated_threat <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> self<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>create_correlated_threat(group) </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> correlated_threats<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>append(correlated_threat) </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">return</span> correlated_threats </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">def</span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">group_alerts_by_similarity</span>(self, alerts, threat similarity"""</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> groups <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> [] </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> processed_alerts <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> set() </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">for</span> i, alert1 <span style="color:#f92672">in</span> enumerate(alerts): </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">if</span> i <span style="color:#f92672">in</span> processed_alerts: </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">continue</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> group <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> [alert1] </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> processed_alerts<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>add(i) </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> alert1_time <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> datetime<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>fromisoformat(alert1[<span style="color:#e6db74">'timestamp'</span>]) </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">for</span> j, alert2 <span style="color:#f92672">in</span> enumerate(alerts[i<span style="color:#f92672">+</span><span style="color:#ae81ff">1</span>:], i<span style="color:#f92672">+</span><span style="color:#ae81ff">1</span>): </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">if</span> j <span style="color:#f92672">in</span> processed_alerts: </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">continue</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> alert2_time <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> datetime<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>fromisoformat(alert2[<span style="color:#e6db74">'timestamp'</span>]) </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> time_diff <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> abs((alert1_time <span style="color:#f92672">-</span> alert2_time)<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>total_seconds()) </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">if</span> (time_diff <span style="color:#f92672"><=</span> time_window <span style="color:#f92672">and</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> alert1[<span style="color:#e6db74">'heuristic'</span>] <span style="color:#f92672">==</span> alert2[<span style="color:#e6db74">'heuristic'</span>] <span style="color:#f92672">and</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> alert1[<span style="color:#e6db74">'source_device'</span>] <span style="color:#f92672">!=</span> alert2[<span style="color:#e6db74">'source_device'</span>]): </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> group<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>append(alert2) </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> processed_alerts<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>add(j) </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> groups<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>append(group) </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">return</span> groups </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">def</span> <span style="color:#a6e22e">consensus_validation</span>(self, correlated_threats): </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#e6db74">"""Validate threats through device consensus"""</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> validated_threats <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> [] </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">for</span> threat <span style="color:#f92672">in</span> correlated_threats: </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> device_count <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> len(set(alert[<span style="color:#e6db74">'source_device'</span>] <span style="color:#66d9ef">for</span> alert <span style="color:#f92672">in</span> threat[<span style="color:#e6db74">'constituent_alerts'</span>])) </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> confidence_scores <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> [alert[<span style="color:#e6db74">'confidence_score'</span>] <span style="color:#66d9ef">for</span> alert <span style="color:#f92672">in</span> threat[<span style="color:#e6db74">'constituent_alerts'</span>]] </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#75715e"># Require minimum number of devices and confidence threshold</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">if</span> (device_count <span style="color:#f92672">>=</span> <span style="color:#ae81ff">2</span> <span style="color:#f92672">and</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> np<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>mean(confidence_scores) <span style="color:#f92672">>=</span> <span style="color:#ae81ff">0.7</span> <span style="color:#f92672">and</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> min(confidence_scores) <span style="color:#f92672">>=</span> <span style="color:#ae81ff">0.5</span>): </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> threat[<span style="color:#e6db74">'validated'</span>] <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">True</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> threat[<span style="color:#e6db74">'consensus_strength'</span>] <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> device_count <span style="color:#f92672">/</span> len(self<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>devices) </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> threat[<span style="color:#e6db74">'confidence_score'</span>] <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> np<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>mean(confidence_scores) </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> validated_threats<span style="color:#f92672">.</span>append(threat) </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">return</span> validated_threats </span></span></code></pre></div><h2 style="color:#e6db74">"\nMemory usage:"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>ps aux | grep rayhunter | awk <span style="color:#e6db74">'{print $4, $6, $11}'</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Check disk I/O</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>echo -e <span style="color:#e6db74">"\nDisk I/O:"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>iotop -a -o -d1 -n3 | grep rayhunter </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Analyze configuration issues</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>echo -e <span style="color:#e6db74">"\nConfiguration analysis:"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#66d9ef">if</span> <span style="color:#f92672">[</span> -f /data/rayhunter/config.toml <span style="color:#f92672">]</span>; <span style="color:#66d9ef">then</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#75715e"># Check for resource-intensive configurations</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> grep -E <span style="color:#e6db74">"test_heuristic.*true|sensitivity.*maximum|debug.*true"</span> /data/rayhunter/config.toml </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#75715e"># Check recording settings</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> grep -E <span style="color:#e6db74">"recording|storage"</span> /data/rayhunter/config.toml </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#66d9ef">else</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> echo <span style="color:#e6db74">"Config file not found!"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#66d9ef">fi</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Check for excessive logging</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>echo -e <span style="color:#e6db74">"\nLog file sizes:"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>du -sh /data/rayhunter/logs/* </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Network performance check</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>echo -e <span style="color:#e6db74">"\nNetwork performance:"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>netstat -i | grep -E <span recommendations</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>echo -e <span style="color:#e6db74">"\n=== Optimization Recommendations ==="</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Check if test heuristic is enabled</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#66d9ef">if</span> grep -q <span style="color:#e6db74">"test_heuristic.*=.*true"</span> /data/rayhunter/config.toml; <span style="color:#66d9ef">then</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> echo <span style="color:#e6db74">"⚠️ Test heuristic is enabled - disable for production use"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#66d9ef">fi</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Check sensitivity settings</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#66d9ef">if</span> grep -q <span style="color:#e6db74">"sensitivity.*=.*\"maximum\""</span> /data/rayhunter/config.toml; <span style="color:#66d9ef">then</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> echo <span style="color:#e6db74">"⚠️ Maximum sensitivity may cause high CPU usage in dense environments"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#66d9ef">fi</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Check storage settings</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>STORAGE_USED<span style="color:#f92672">=</span><span style="color:#66d9ef">$(</span>df -h /data/rayhunter | awk <span style="color:#e6db74">'NR==2 {print $5}'</span> | sed <span style="color:#e6db74">'s/%//'</span><span style="color:#66d9ef">)</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#66d9ef">if</span> <span style="color:#f92672">[</span> <span style="color:#e6db74">"</span>$STORAGE_USED<span style="color:#e6db74">"</span> -gt <span style="color:#ae81ff">80</span> <span style="color:#f92672">]</span>; <span style="color:#66d9ef">then</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> echo <span style="color:#e6db74">"⚠️ Storage usage is </span><span style="color:#e6db74">${</span>STORAGE_USED<span style="color:#e6db74">}</span><span style="color:#e6db74">% - clean up old recordings"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#66d9ef">fi</span> </span></span></code></pre></div><p><strong>Memory Leak Detection and Resolution</strong>:</p> <div class="highlight"><pre tabindex="0" style="color:#f8f8f2;background-color:#272822;-moz-tab-size:4;-o-tab-size:4;tab-size:4;-webkit-text-size-adjust:none;"><code class="language-bash" data-lang="bash"><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e">#!/bin/bash </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Memory leak detection and automatic style="color:#ae81ff">80</span> <span style="color:#75715e"># 80% memory usage threshold</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>LOG_FILE<span style="color:#f92672">=</span><span style="color:#e6db74">"/var/log/rayhunter/memory_monitor.log"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>monitor_memory<span style="color:#f92672">()</span> <span style="color:#f92672">{</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">while</span> true; <span style="color:#66d9ef">do</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#75715e"># Get RayHunter memory usage</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> MEMORY_USAGE<span style="color:#f92672">=</span><span style="color:#66d9ef">$(</span>ps aux | grep rayhunter-daemon | grep -v grep | awk <span style="color:#e6db74">'{print $4}'</span><span style="color:#66d9ef">)</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">if</span> <span style="color:#f92672">[</span> ! -z <span style="color:#e6db74">"</span>$MEMORY_USAGE<span style="color:#e6db74">"</span> <span style="color:#f92672">]</span>; <span style="color:#66d9ef">then</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> MEMORY_PERCENT<span style="color:#f92672">=</span><span style="color:#66d9ef">$(</span>echo <span style="color:#e6db74">"</span>$MEMORY_USAGE<span style="color:#e6db74">"</span> | cut -d. -f1<span style="color:#66d9ef">)</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> echo <span style="color:#e6db74">"</span><span style="color:#66d9ef">$(</span>date<span style="color:#66d9ef">)</span><span style="color:#e6db74">: RayHunter memory usage: </span><span style="color:#e6db74">${</span>MEMORY_USAGE<span style="color:#e6db74">}</span><span style="color:#e6db74">%"</span> >> <span style="color:#e6db74">"</span>$LOG_FILE<span style="color:#e6db74">"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">if</span> <span style="color:#f92672">[</span> <span style="color:#e6db74">"</span>$MEMORY_PERCENT<span style="color:#e6db74">"</span> -gt <span style="color:#e6db74">"</span>$MEMORY_THRESHOLD<span style="color:#e6db74">"</span> <span style="color:#f92672">]</span>; <span style="color:#66d9ef">then</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> echo <span style="color:#e6db74">"</span><span style="color:#66d9ef">$(</span>date<span style="color:#66d9ef">)</span><span style="color:#e6db74">: High memory usage detected: </span><span style="color:#e6db74">${</span>MEMORY_USAGE<span style="color:#e6db74">}</span><span style="color:#e6db74">%"</span> >> <span style="color:#e6db74">"</span>$LOG_FILE<span style="color:#e6db74">"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#75715e"># Attempt graceful restart</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> echo <span style="color:#e6db74">"</span><span style="color:#66d9ef">$(</span>date<span style="color:#66d9ef">)</span><span style="color:#e6db74">: Attempting graceful restart..."</span> >> <span style="color:#e6db74">"</span>$LOG_FILE<span style="color:#e6db74">"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> systemctl restart rayhunter_daemon </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> sleep <span style="color:#ae81ff">30</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#75715e"># Verify restart was successful</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">if</span> pgrep rayhunter-daemon > /dev/null; <span style="color:#66d9ef">then</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> echo <span style="color:#e6db74">"</span><span style="color:#66d9ef">$(</span>date<span style="color:#66d9ef">)</span><span style="color:#e6db74">: Graceful restart successful"</span> >> <span style="color:#e6db74">"</span>$LOG_FILE<span style="color:#e6db74">"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">else</span> </span></span><span failed, attempting force restart"</span> >> <span style="color:#e6db74">"</span>$LOG_FILE<span style="color:#e6db74">"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> killall -9 rayhunter-daemon </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> systemctl start rayhunter_daemon </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">fi</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">fi</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">fi</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">done</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#f92672">}</span> </span></span><span class="language-bash" data-lang="bash"><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e">#!/bin/bash </span></span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Advanced RayHunter connectivity troubleshooting</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>echo <span style="color:#e6db74">"=== RayHunter </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>echo <span style="color:#e6db74">"1. Basic connectivity check:"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>ping -c <span style="color:#ae81ff">3</span> 8.8.8.8 > /dev/null 2>&<span style="color:#ae81ff">1</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#66d9ef">if</span> <span style="color:#f92672">[</span> $? -eq <span style="color:#ae81ff">0</span> <span style="color:#f92672">]</span>; <span style="color:#66d9ef">then</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> echo <span style="color:#e6db74">"✓ Internet connectivity: OK"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#66d9ef">else</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> echo <span style="color:#e6db74">"✗ Internet connectivity: FAILED"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#66d9ef">fi</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Check cellular connection</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>echo -e <span style="color:#e6db74">"\n2. Cellular connection analysis:"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#66d9ef">if</span> command -v qmicli &> /dev/null; <span style="color:#66d9ef">then</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#75715e"># Check modem status</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> qmicli -d /dev/cdc-wdm0 --dms-get-operating-mode </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> qmicli -d /dev/cdc-wdm0 --nas-get-signal-strength </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> qmicli -d /dev/cdc-wdm0 --nas-get-serving-system </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#66d9ef">else</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> echo <span style="color:#e6db74">"qmicli not available - using alternative methods"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#75715e"># Check network interfaces</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> ip addr show | grep -A <span style="color:#ae81ff">10</span> wwan </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#75715e"># Check routing</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> ip route show </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#66d9ef">fi</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Check RayHunter web interface</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span>echo -e <span style="color:#e6db74">"\n3. RayHunter web interface check:"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#66d9ef">for</span> port in <span style="color:#ae81ff">8080</span> 80; <span style="color:#66d9ef">do</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">if</span> netstat -tuln | grep <span style="color:#e6db74">":</span>$port<span style="color:#e6db74"> "</span> > /dev/null; <span style="color:#66d9ef">then</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> echo <span style="color:#e6db74">"✓ Port </span>$port<span style="color:#e6db74"> is listening"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#75715e"># Test HTTP response</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span style="color:#66d9ef">if</span> curl -s -o /dev/null -w <span style="color:#e6db74">"%{http_code}"</span> <span style="color:#e6db74">"http://localhost:</span>$port<span style="color:#e6db74">"</span> | grep -q <span style="color:#e6db74">"200\|302"</span>; Advanced network analysis:"</span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span><span style="color:#75715e"># Check APN settings</span> analysis[<span style="color:#e6db74">'environmental_correlation'</span>][env_type] <span style="color:#f92672">=</span> { </span></span><span style="display:flex;"><span> <span
